<content>1: const { pool } = require("../../db/pool");
2: const ApiError = require("../../errors/api-error");
3: const { writeAuditEvent } = require("../../services/audit-log");
4: const {
5:   ensureUserNotBlacklisted,
6:   checkSensitiveWords,
7:   enforceDailyPublishCap
8: } = require("./moderation.service");
9: const { flagSharedDeviceRisk, handleUpheldViolation, refreshGovernanceAnalyticsSnapshot } = require("./risk.service");
10: const { canCreateFollowup, canAppeal } = require("./review.rules");
11: 
12: async function createReview({ userId, payload, requestId }) {
13:   await ensureUserNotBlacklisted(userId);
14:   await enforceDailyPublishCap(userId);
15:   await checkSensitiveWords(payload.reviewText);
16: 
17:   const connection = await pool.getConnection();
18:   try {
19:     await connection.beginTransaction();
20: 
21:     const [orderRows] = await connection.query(
22:       `
23:         SELECT id, order_status
24:         FROM orders
25:         WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
26:         LIMIT 1
27:         FOR UPDATE
28:       `,
29:       [payload.orderId, userId]
30:     );
31: 
32:     if (!orderRows.length) {
33:       throw new ApiError(404, "ORDER_NOT_FOUND", "Order not found for user");
34:     }
35:     if (orderRows[0].order_status !== "completed") {
36:       throw new ApiError(400, "ORDER_NOT_COMPLETED", "Only completed orders can be reviewed");
37:     }
38: 
39:     const [existingRows] = await connection.query("SELECT id FROM reviews WHERE order_id = ? AND user_id = ? LIMIT 1", [
40:       payload.orderId,
41:       userId
42:     ]);
43:     if (existingRows.length) {
44:       throw new ApiError(409, "REVIEW_ALREADY_EXISTS", "One primary review per order is allowed");
45:     }
46: 
47:     const [insert] = await connection.query(
48:       `
49:         INSERT INTO reviews (order_id, user_id, rating, review_state, anonymous_display, review_text, published_at)
50:         VALUES (?, ?, ?, 'published', ?, ?, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
51:       `,
52:       [payload.orderId, userId, payload.rating, payload.anonymousDisplay ? 1 : 0, payload.reviewText]
53:     );
54: 
55:     for (const dimension of payload.dimensionScores) {
56:       const [dimensionRows] = await connection.query("SELECT id FROM review_dimension_configs WHERE id = ? AND is_active = 1 LIMIT 1", [
57:         dimension.dimensionConfigId
58:       ]);
59:       if (!dimensionRows.length) {
60:         throw new ApiError(400, "INVALID_DIMENSION", `Dimension ${dimension.dimensionConfigId} is not active`);
61:       }
62: 
63:       await connection.query(
64:         `
65:           INSERT INTO review_dimension_scores (review_id, dimension_config_id, score)
66:           VALUES (?, ?, ?)
67:         `,
68:         [insert.insertId, dimension.dimensionConfigId, dimension.score]
69:       );
70:     }
71: 
72:     await connection.query(
73:       `
74:         INSERT INTO moderation_events (review_id, actor_user_id, event_type, decision, notes)
75:         VALUES (?, ?, 'review_publish', 'allow', 'Passed baseline moderation checks')
76:       `,
77:       [insert.insertId, userId]
78:     );
79: 
80:     await connection.commit();
81: 
82:     await flagSharedDeviceRisk(userId);
83:     await refreshGovernanceAnalyticsSnapshot();
84:     await writeAuditEvent({
85:       actorUserId: userId,
86:       eventType: "review.created",
87:       entityType: "review",
88:       entityId: String(insert.insertId),
89:       requestId,
90:       payload: { orderId: payload.orderId }
91:     });
92: 
93:     const [rows] = await pool.query("SELECT * FROM reviews WHERE id = ? LIMIT 1", [insert.insertId]);
94:     return rows[0];
95:   } catch (error) {
96:     await connection.rollback();
97:     throw error;
98:   } finally {
99:     connection.release();
100:   }
101: }
102: 
103: async function addFollowup({ userId, reviewId, followupText, requestId }) {
104:   await ensureUserNotBlacklisted(userId);
105:   await enforceDailyPublishCap(userId);
106:   await checkSensitiveWords(followupText);
107: 
108:   const connection = await pool.getConnection();
109:   try {
110:     await connection.beginTransaction();
111: 
112:     const [reviewRows] = await connection.query("SELECT * FROM reviews WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ? LIMIT 1 FOR UPDATE", [reviewId, userId]);
113:     if (!reviewRows.length) {
114:       throw new ApiError(404, "REVIEW_NOT_FOUND", "Review not found for user");
115:     }
116: 
117:     const review = reviewRows[0];
118:     if (!canCreateFollowup(review.published_at)) {
119:       throw new ApiError(400, "FOLLOWUP_WINDOW_EXPIRED", "Follow-up review is only allowed within 30 days");
120:     }
121: 
122:     const [existingRows] = await connection.query("SELECT id FROM review_followups WHERE review_id = ? LIMIT 1", [reviewId]);
123:     if (existingRows.length) {
124:       throw new ApiError(409, "FOLLOWUP_ALREADY_EXISTS", "Only one follow-up review is allowed");
125:     }
126: 
127:     const [insert] = await connection.query(
128:       `
129:         INSERT INTO review_followups (review_id, user_id, followup_text)
130:         VALUES (?, ?, ?)
131:       `,
132:       [reviewId, userId, followupText]
133:     );
134: 
135:     await connection.commit();
136: 
137:     await writeAuditEvent({
138:       actorUserId: userId,
139:       eventType: "review.followup.created",
140:       entityType: "review_followup",
141:       entityId: String(insert.insertId),
142:       requestId,
143:       payload: { reviewId }
144:     });
145: 
146:     const [rows] = await pool.query("SELECT * FROM review_followups WHERE id = ? LIMIT 1", [insert.insertId]);
147:     return rows[0];
148:   } catch (error) {
149:     await connection.rollback();
150:     throw error;
151:   } finally {
152:     connection.release();
153:   }
154: }
155: 
156: async function getReviewDetail({ reviewId, requester }) {
157:   const [reviewRows] = await pool.query(
158:     `
159:       SELECT r.*, u.username, p.display_name
160:       FROM reviews r
161:       JOIN users u ON u.id = r.user_id
162:       LEFT JOIN user_profiles p ON p.user_id = r.user_id
163:       WHERE r.id = ?
164:       LIMIT 1
165:     `,
166:     [reviewId]
167:   );
168:   if (!reviewRows.length) {
169:     throw new ApiError(404, "REVIEW_NOT_FOUND", "Review not found");
170:   }
171: 
172:   const review = reviewRows[0];
173:   const hidden = review.review_state === "under_arbitration";
174:   const displayName = review.anonymous_display ? "Anonymous User" : review.display_name || review.username;
175: 
176:   const [dimensionRows] = await pool.query(
177:     `
178:       SELECT ds.dimension_config_id, ds.score, dc.label, dc.key_name
179:       FROM review_dimension_scores ds
180:       JOIN review_dimension_configs dc ON dc.id = ds.dimension_config_id
181:       WHERE ds.review_id = ?
182:     `,
183:     [reviewId]
184:   );
185: 
186:   const [imageRows] = await pool.query(
187:     "SELECT id, mime_type, file_size_bytes, sort_order FROM review_images WHERE review_id = ? ORDER BY sort_order ASC, id ASC",
188:     [reviewId]
189:   );
190: 
191:   const [followupRows] = await pool.query("SELECT * FROM review_followups WHERE review_id = ? LIMIT 1", [reviewId]);
192:   const [replyRows] = await pool.query(
193:     `
194:       SELECT rr.id, rr.parent_reply_id, rr.reply_text, rr.created_at, rr.author_role, u.username
195:       FROM review_replies rr
196:       JOIN users u ON u.id = rr.author_user_id
197:       WHERE rr.review_id = ?
198:       ORDER BY rr.id ASC
199:     `,
200:     [reviewId]
201:   );
202:   const [appealRows] = await pool.query("SELECT * FROM appeals WHERE review_id = ? ORDER BY id DESC", [reviewId]);
203:   const appeal = appealRows[0] || null;
204: 
205:   let timeline = [];
206:   if (appeal) {
207:     const [timelineRows] = await pool.query(
208:       "SELECT event_type, event_status, event_note, created_at FROM appeal_timeline_events WHERE appeal_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC",
209:       [appeal.id]
210:     );
211:     timeline = timelineRows;
212:   }
213: 
214:   const canAppealNow = requester && requester.id === review.user_id && canAppeal(review.published_at) && (!appeal || appeal.appeal_status === "resolved");
215: 
216:   return {
217:     id: review.id,
218:     orderId: review.order_id,
219:     userId: review.user_id,
220:     rating: review.rating,
221:     reviewState: review.review_state,
222:     displayName,
223:     anonymousDisplay: Boolean(review.anonymous_display),
224:     reviewText: hidden ? "Content hidden during arbitration" : review.review_text,
225:     publishedAt: review.published_at,
226:     dimensions: dimensionRows,
227:     images: hidden ? [] : imageRows,
228:     followup: hidden ? null : followupRows[0] || null,
229:     replies: replyRows,
230:     appeal: appeal
231:       ? {
232:           id: appeal.id,
233:           status: appeal.appeal_status,
234:           submittedAt: appeal.submitted_at,
235:           resolvedAt: appeal.resolved_at,
236:           timeline
237:         }
238:       : null,
239:     canAppeal: Boolean(canAppealNow)
240:   };
241: }
242: 
243: async function createAppeal({ userId, reviewId, reason, requestId }) {
244:   const connection = await pool.getConnection();
245:   try {
246:     await connection.beginTransaction();
247: 
248:     const [reviewRows] = await connection.query("SELECT * FROM reviews WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ? LIMIT 1 FOR UPDATE", [reviewId, userId]);
249:     if (!reviewRows.length) {
250:       throw new ApiError(404, "REVIEW_NOT_FOUND", "Review not found for user");
251:     }
252: 
253:     const review = reviewRows[0];
254:     if (!canAppeal(review.published_at)) {
255:       throw new ApiError(400, "APPEAL_WINDOW_EXPIRED", "Appeals can be submitted within 7 days of publishing");
256:     }
257: 
258:     const [existingAppealRows] = await connection.query(
259:       "SELECT id FROM appeals WHERE review_id = ? AND appeal_status IN ('submitted', 'under_review') LIMIT 1",
260:       [reviewId]
261:     );
262:     if (existingAppealRows.length) {
263:       throw new ApiError(409, "APPEAL_ALREADY_OPEN", "An appeal is already active for this review");
264:     }
265: 
266:     const [insert] = await connection.query(
267:       `
268:         INSERT INTO appeals (review_id, appellant_user_id, appeal_status, appeal_reason)
269:         VALUES (?, ?, 'submitted', ?)
270:       `,
271:       [reviewId, userId, reason]
272:     );
273: 
274:     await connection.query(
275:       `
276:         INSERT INTO appeal_timeline_events (appeal_id, event_type, event_status, event_note, created_by_user_id)
277:         VALUES (?, 'appeal_submitted', 'submitted', ?, ?)
278:       `,
279:       [insert.insertId, "Appeal submitted", userId]
280:     );
281: 
282:     await connection.query("UPDATE reviews SET review_state = 'under_arbitration' WHERE id = ?", [reviewId]);
283:     await connection.commit();
284: 
285:     await writeAuditEvent({
286:       actorUserId: userId,
287:       eventType: "appeal.created",
288:       entityType: "appeal",
289:       entityId: String(insert.insertId),
290:       requestId,
291:       payload: { reviewId }
292:     });
293: 
294:     const [rows] = await pool.query("SELECT * FROM appeals WHERE id = ? LIMIT 1", [insert.insertId]);
295:     return rows[0];
296:   } catch (error) {
297:     await connection.rollback();
298:     throw error;
299:   } finally {
300:     connection.release();
301:   }
302: }
303: 
304: async function addReply({ actorUser, reviewId, parentReplyId, replyText, requestId }) {
305:   await checkSensitiveWords(replyText);
306: 
307:   const [reviewRows] = await pool.query("SELECT id FROM reviews WHERE id = ? LIMIT 1", [reviewId]);
308:   if (!reviewRows.length) {
309:     throw new ApiError(404, "REVIEW_NOT_FOUND", "Review not found");
310:   }
311: 
312:   if (parentReplyId) {
313:     const [parentRows] = await pool.query("SELECT id FROM review_replies WHERE id = ? AND review_id = ? LIMIT 1", [parentReplyId, reviewId]);
314:     if (!parentRows.length) {
315:       throw new ApiError(400, "INVALID_PARENT_REPLY", "Parent reply does not belong to this review");
316:     }
317:   }
318: 
319:   const role = actorUser.roles.includes("coach")
320:     ? "coach"
321:     : actorUser.roles.includes("support")
322:       ? "support"
323:       : actorUser.roles.includes("admin")
324:         ? "admin"
325:         : "user";
326: 
327:   const [insert] = await pool.query(
328:     `
329:       INSERT INTO review_replies (review_id, parent_reply_id, author_user_id, author_role, reply_text)
330:       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
331:     `,
332:     [reviewId, parentReplyId || null, actorUser.id, role, replyText]
333:   );
334: 
335:   await writeAuditEvent({
336:     actorUserId: actorUser.id,
337:     eventType: "review.reply.created",
338:     entityType: "review_reply",
339:     entityId: String(insert.insertId),
340:     requestId,
341:     payload: { reviewId }
342:   });
343: 
344:   const [rows] = await pool.query("SELECT * FROM review_replies WHERE id = ? LIMIT 1", [insert.insertId]);
345:   return rows[0];
346: }
347: 
348: async function listUserReviews(userId) {
349:   const [rows] = await pool.query(
350:     `
351:       SELECT r.id, r.order_id, r.rating, r.review_state, r.anonymous_display, r.review_text, r.published_at, r.created_at,
352:              o.order_status
353:       FROM reviews r
354:       JOIN orders o ON o.id = r.order_id
355:       WHERE r.user_id = ?
356:       ORDER BY r.id DESC
357:     `,
358:     [userId]
359:   );
360: 
361:   if (!rows.length) {
362:     return [];
363:   }
364: 
365:   const reviewIds = rows.map((row) => row.id);
366:   const [imageRows] = await pool.query(
367:     `
368:       SELECT review_id, id, mime_type, sort_order
369:       FROM review_images
370:       WHERE review_id IN (?)
371:       ORDER BY review_id ASC, sort_order ASC, id ASC
372:     `,
373:     [reviewIds]
374:   );
375: 
376:   const [followupRows] = await pool.query(
377:     `
378:       SELECT id, review_id, followup_text, created_at
379:       FROM review_followups
380:       WHERE review_id IN (?)
381:     `,
382:     [reviewIds]
383:   );
384: 
385:   const imagesByReview = new Map();
386:   for (const image of imageRows) {
387:     if (!imagesByReview.has(image.review_id)) {
388:       imagesByReview.set(image.review_id, []);
389:     }
390:     imagesByReview.get(image.review_id).push({
391:       id: image.id,
392:       mimeType: image.mime_type,
393:       url: `/api/v1/reviews/images/${image.id}`
394:     });
395:   }
396: 
397:   const followupByReview = new Map();
398:   for (const followup of followupRows) {
399:     followupByReview.set(followup.review_id, {
400:       id: followup.id,
401:       followupText: followup.followup_text,
402:       createdAt: followup.created_at
403:     });
404:   }
405: 
406:   return rows.map((row) => {
407:     const hidden = row.review_state === "under_arbitration";
408:     const images = hidden ? [] : imagesByReview.get(row.id) || [];
409:     return {
410:       ...row,
411:       image_count: images.length,
412:       image_previews: images.slice(0, 3),
413:       followup: hidden ? null : followupByReview.get(row.id) || null
414:     };
415:   });
416: }
417: 
418: async function listAppealsForStaff(status = null) {
419:   const [rows] = await pool.query(
420:     `
421:       SELECT a.*, r.user_id AS review_author_user_id, r.review_state, r.review_text
422:       FROM appeals a
423:       JOIN reviews r ON r.id = a.review_id
424:       ${status ? "WHERE a.appeal_status = ?" : ""}
425:       ORDER BY a.id DESC
426:       LIMIT 500
427:     `,
428:     status ? [status] : []
429:   );
430: 
431:   return rows;
432: }
433: 
434: async function updateAppealStatus({ actorUserId, appealId, appealStatus, note, requestId }) {
435:   const connection = await pool.getConnection();
436:   try {
437:     await connection.beginTransaction();
438: 
439:     const [appealRows] = await connection.query("SELECT * FROM appeals WHERE id = ? LIMIT 1 FOR UPDATE", [appealId]);
440:     if (!appealRows.length) {
441:       throw new ApiError(404, "APPEAL_NOT_FOUND", "Appeal not found");
442:     }
443: 
444:     const appeal = appealRows[0];
445:     await connection.query(
446:       "UPDATE appeals SET appeal_status = ?, resolved_at = CASE WHEN ? IN ('upheld','rejected','resolved') THEN CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ELSE resolved_at END WHERE id = ?",
447:       [appealStatus, appealStatus, appealId]
448:     );
449: 
450:     await connection.query(
451:       `
452:         INSERT INTO appeal_timeline_events (appeal_id, event_type, event_status, event_note, created_by_user_id)
453:         VALUES (?, 'status_update', ?, ?, ?)
454:       `,
455:       [appealId, appealStatus, note || null, actorUserId]
456:     );
457: 
458:     if (appealStatus === "under_review") {
459:       await connection.query("UPDATE reviews SET review_state = 'under_arbitration' WHERE id = ?", [appeal.review_id]);
460:     } else if (appealStatus === "upheld") {
461:       await connection.query("UPDATE reviews SET review_state = 'hidden' WHERE id = ?", [appeal.review_id]);
462:     } else {
463:       await connection.query("UPDATE reviews SET review_state = 'published' WHERE id = ?", [appeal.review_id]);
464:     }
465: 
466:     await connection.commit();
467: 
468:     if (appealStatus === "upheld") {
469:       const [reviewRows] = await pool.query("SELECT user_id FROM reviews WHERE id = ? LIMIT 1", [appeal.review_id]);
470:       if (reviewRows.length) {
471:         await handleUpheldViolation(reviewRows[0].user_id);
472:       }
473:     }
474: 
475:     await refreshGovernanceAnalyticsSnapshot();
476:     await writeAuditEvent({
477:       actorUserId,
478:       eventType: "appeal.status.updated",
479:       entityType: "appeal",
480:       entityId: String(appealId),
481:       requestId,
482:       payload: { appealStatus }
483:     });
484: 
485:     const [rows] = await pool.query("SELECT * FROM appeals WHERE id = ? LIMIT 1", [appealId]);
486:     return rows[0];
487:   } catch (error) {
488:     await connection.rollback();
489:     throw error;
490:   } finally {
491:     connection.release();
492:   }
493: }
494: 
495: module.exports = {
496:   createReview,
497:   addFollowup,
498:   listUserReviews,
499:   listAppealsForStaff,
500:   getReviewDetail,
501:   createAppeal,
502:   addReply,
503:   updateAppealStatus
504: };

content.text:
Done ΓÇö I completed a deeper modularization of the review domain while preserving the existing API surface.

Refactor highlights (no route contract changes) centered on splitting `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.core.service.js` monolith into focused modules and keeping `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.service.js` as the compatibility facade.

**Module Changelog**
- **Shared utilities**
  - Added transaction + shared helpers in `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.shared.js`.
  - Extracted:
    - `withTransaction` for consistent transaction handling
    - `resolveReplyAuthorRole` for deterministic role mapping
    - `writeReviewAudit` for centralized audit writes

- **Review creation**
  - Added `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.create.service.js`.
  - Responsibilities:
    - review creation orchestration
    - dimension score persistence (`persistDimensionScores`)
    - moderation event insert
    - post-commit risk + analytics + audit hooks

- **Follow-up management**
  - Added `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.followup.service.js`.
  - Keeps window checks, duplicate prevention, ownership guard, and audit semantics unchanged.

- **Read/detail aggregation + list/query helpers**
  - Added `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.read.service.js`.
  - Responsibilities:
    - review detail composition
    - user review list composition
    - extracted mappers:
      - `toReviewImagePreviews`
      - `toFollowupsByReview`

- **Appeals workflow**
  - Added `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.appeals.service.js`.
  - Responsibilities:
    - appeal creation
    - staff appeal listing
    - appeal status transitions
    - transition mapping helper: `reviewStateFromAppealStatus`
    - upheld-violation escalation + analytics refresh + audit

- **Replies workflow**
  - Added `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.replies.service.js`.
  - Responsibilities:
    - reply validation
    - optional parent reply validation
    - role resolution + persistence + audit

- **Compatibility facade**
  - Replaced monolith implementation in `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.core.service.js` with exports-only composition of the new modules.
  - `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.service.js` remains stable and continues to export the same domain entry points (plus image service), so routes/controllers donΓÇÖt need changes.

**Public Surface Stability**
- Existing routes still call the same high-level exports via `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.service.js`.
- Error codes/messages/statuses were preserved in the extracted modules.
- No schema changes and no API contract breaks introduced.

**New/Expanded Tests**
- Added appeal transition coverage in `backend/tests/reviews-appeals.service.test.js`:
  - under_review transition behavior
  - upheld transition + risk escalation hook
  - transition mapping helper behavior
- Added detail composition coverage in `backend/tests/reviews-read.service.test.js`:
  - hidden/arbitration masking behavior
  - owner canAppeal behavior within window
- Added follow-up/reply edge-case coverage in `backend/tests/reviews-write-edge-cases.test.js`:
  - follow-up window expiry
  - duplicate follow-up rejection
  - invalid parent reply rejection
  - deterministic reply role resolution

**Quality Gates**
- `npm --prefix backend test` Γ£à
- `npm test` (backend + frontend) Γ£à
- `npm run build` Γ£à
- Lint/typecheck scripts are still not defined in current package manifests, so those specific gates cannot be executed as standalone commands in this repo state.

**Residual Technical Debt**
- The review read module still contains several direct query blocks that could be further decomposed into a repository/query layer if desired.
- Audit event payload construction is centralized now but still stringly-typed; a typed event factory pattern would improve long-term safety if the codebase later moves to TypeScript.
- Existing broader workspace changes (from prior tasks) remain in git status; this pass only focused on review-domain modularization and matching tests.

## Objective
Add a complete, runnable, repeatable testing framework that satisfies:
1) API functional testing requirements
- Cover core external API interfaces with normal-parameter calls.
- Add abnormal scenario tests for:
  - missing required parameters
  - invalid parameter format/type
  - insufficient permission / unauthorized access
- For data-changing APIs, verify before/after state correctness (not just status code).
2) Test execution mode + output requirements
- Create mandatory directories at project root:
  - `unit_tests/`
  - `API_tests/`
- Organize test scripts/resources under those directories.
- Provide unified one-click shell execution from project root:
  - `run_tests.sh`
- Ensure tests are repeatable (idempotent setup/cleanup and deterministic outputs).
- Output clear per-test results:
  - test name
  - status (PASS/FAIL)
  - failure reason
  - useful error log snippet
- Output final summary:
  - total tests
  - passed
  - failed
- Exit non-zero if any test fails.
3) Acceptance judgment alignment
- Ensure test coverage includes the vast majority of functional buttons/main business paths via API coverage.
- If coverage is partial, explicitly identify gaps and include TODO markers in output summary.
---
## Implementation requirements
### A. Directory and script structure (mandatory)
Create this structure under `repo/`:
- `unit_tests/`
  - test runner scripts/resources for unit tests
- `API_tests/`
  - API functional test scripts/resources
- `run_tests.sh`
  - master script that runs both suites and prints consolidated report
### B. Unit tests integration
- Reuse and execute existing backend/frontend unit tests from the new `unit_tests/` runner.
- If needed, add additional unit tests for uncovered critical logic.
- Standardize output format to PASS/FAIL per logical test group.
### C. API functional tests
Implement API test scripts in `API_tests/` to cover:
- auth/login and permission-sensitive endpoints
- at least one happy path per major module (hiring/support/inventory/compliance)
- abnormal requests (400/401/403/404/409 where applicable)
- state-change validation:
  - fetch pre-state
  - perform request
  - fetch post-state
  - assert expected delta
### D. Runner behavior (`run_tests.sh`)
- Run unit tests then API tests.
- Continue running remaining tests after individual failures (collect all failures).
- Print human-readable and CI-friendly output.
- At end print strict summary block:
  - `TOTAL=`
  - `PASSED=`
  - `FAILED=`
- Return exit code 1 when FAILED > 0, else 0.
### E. Documentation
Update `README.md` with:
- prerequisites for test execution
- one-click command: `./run_tests.sh`
- sample output format
- meaning of exit codes
---
## Technical constraints
- Use shell scripts as required.
- Keep scripts portable and easy to read.
- Avoid Docker-only execution in the scripts unless optional and clearly marked.
- Keep changes minimal but acceptance-compliant.

After implementation, provide:
1) list of created/updated files

3) sample final summary output format

5) exact command to run acceptance tests

<content>1: # TrailForge repo
2: 
3: This repository contains TrailForge as an offline-ready repo project with a Vue frontend, Koa backend, MySQL storage, background worker processing, feed ingestion, governance workflows, and analytics.
4: 
5: ## Services
6: 
7: - `frontend` - Vue 3 + Vite role-aware application
8: - `backend` - Koa API with auth/RBAC, validation, auditing, domain services, analytics, and ingestion APIs
9: - `worker` - queue worker for payment/order sweeps and ingestion jobs
10: - `mysql` - MySQL 8.4 data store
11: 
12: ## Ports
13: 
14: - Frontend: `5173`
15: - Backend API: `3000`
16: - MySQL: `3306`
17: 
18: ## Primary Startup Path
19: 
20: ```bash
21: docker compose up --build
22: ```
23: 
24: This is the default and recommended startup flow.
25: 
26: When started from root compose, backend automatically applies migrations and seeds before serving traffic. No hidden manual initialization steps are required for a clean local boot.
27: 
28: ## Quick Demo Credentials (Seeded)
29: 
30: From `backend/seeds/002_roles_users_seed.js`:
31: 
32: | Role | Username | Password | Email |
33: |---|---|---|---|
34: | Admin | `admin` | `admin12345` | `admin@trailforge.local` |
35: | Coach | `coach1` | `coach12345` | `coach1@trailforge.local` |
36: | Support Agent | `support1` | `support12345` | `support1@trailforge.local` |
37: | Regular User | `athlete1` | `athlete12345` | `athlete1@trailforge.local` |
38: 
39: 
40: ## Optional Local Scripts
41: 
42: ```bash
43: npm run up
44: npm run down
45: npm run logs
46: npm run migrate
47: npm run migrate:down
48: npm run seed
49: npm run test
50: npm run test:integration:db
51: ```
52: 
53: ## Verification Steps
54: 
55: 1. Start stack:
56: 
57: ```bash
58: docker compose up --build
59: ```
60: 
61: 2. Verify backend health:
62: 
63: ```bash
64: curl http://localhost:3000/health
65: ```
66: 
67: Expected response includes `success: true` and `data.status: "ok"`.
68: 
69: 3. Verify auth foundation:
70: 
71: ```bash
72: curl -c /tmp/trailforge-session.cookies -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"username":"admin","password":"admin12345"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/auth/login
73: curl -b /tmp/trailforge-session.cookies http://localhost:3000/api/v1/auth/me
74: curl -b /tmp/trailforge-session.cookies http://localhost:3000/api/v1/admin/test
75: ```
76: 
77: Expected behavior:
78: 
79: - Login returns a signed session cookie.
80: - `/api/v1/auth/me` returns current user data.
81: - `/api/v1/admin/test` is allowed for admin role and denied for non-admin users.
82: 
83: 4. Verify frontend app shell:
84: 
85: - Open `http://localhost:5173`
86: - Confirm TrailForge app shell renders and API base URL is shown.
87: 
88: 5. Verify database connectivity:
89: 
90: - Check backend logs for successful MySQL check at startup.
91: 
92: 6. Run full test pass:
93: 
94: ```bash
95: npm run test
96: ```
97: 
98: ## Backend Verification Workflows
99: 
100: ### Default fast/local backend tests (no DB required)
101: 
102: ```bash
103: npm --prefix backend install
104: npm --prefix backend test
105: ```
106: 
107: Expected outcome:
108: 
109: - Vitest completes successfully in local environments even when MySQL is unavailable.
110: - Refund authorization regression coverage is still exercised (`401`, `403`, privileged success path).
111: 
112: ### DB-backed refund integration tests (opt-in)
113: 
114: Prerequisites:
115: 
116: - MySQL is running and reachable by backend env vars.
117: - Schema migrations and seeds are already applied.
118: 
119: Run integration coverage explicitly:
120: 
121: ```bash
122: npm --prefix backend run test:integration:db
123: ```
124: 
125: Expected outcome:
126: 
127: - `backend/tests/refund-persistence.integration.test.js` runs (not skipped).
128: - Authorized refund request persists side effects end-to-end:
129:   - refund row created,
130:   - order refunded amount and status updated,
131:   - payment status updated,
132:   - ledger refund entry created.
133: 
134: If MySQL is unavailable, this command fails fast with a prerequisite message instead of running the baseline suite.
135: 
136: ### Docker checklist (full stack)
137: 
138: 1. Start services:
139: 
140: ```bash
141: docker compose up --build
142: ```
143: 
144: 2. Health check:
145: 
146: ```bash
147: curl http://localhost:3000/health
148: ```
149: 
150: 3. Refund authorization checks:
151: 
152: ```bash
153: # Unauthenticated should be 401
154: curl -i -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":0.01,"reason":"test","idempotencyKey":"unauth-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/1/refunds
155: 
156: # Authenticated regular user should be 403
157: curl -c /tmp/trailforge-user.cookies -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"username":"athlete1","password":"athlete12345"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/auth/login
158: curl -i -b /tmp/trailforge-user.cookies -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":0.01,"reason":"test","idempotencyKey":"user-forbidden-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/1/refunds
159: 
160: # Create a refundable fixture order/payment in MySQL (returns new order id)
161: docker exec trailforge-mysql mysql -utrailforge -ptrailforge -D trailforge -Nse "SET @uid=(SELECT id FROM users WHERE username='athlete1' LIMIT 1); SET @pid=(SELECT id FROM users WHERE username='support1' LIMIT 1); INSERT INTO courses_services (kind,title,description,provider_user_id,status) VALUES ('service', CONCAT('Docker Refund Fixture ', UNIX_TIMESTAMP()), 'docker refund verification fixture', @pid, 'active'); SET @csid=LAST_INSERT_ID(); INSERT INTO orders (user_id,course_service_id,order_type,order_status,total_amount_cents,paid_amount_cents,refunded_amount_cents,currency,idempotency_key) VALUES (@uid,@csid,'service','paid',1000,1000,0,'USD', CONCAT('docker-refund-order-', UNIX_TIMESTAMP())); SET @oid=LAST_INSERT_ID(); INSERT INTO payments (order_id,provider,provider_txn_id,payment_status,amount_cents,signature_valid,raw_payload,confirmed_at) VALUES (@oid,'wechat_pay', CONCAT('docker-txn-', UNIX_TIMESTAMP()),'confirmed',1000,1,JSON_OBJECT('source','docker-refund-check'),CURRENT_TIMESTAMP); SELECT @oid;"
162: 
163: # Authenticated support/admin should succeed on that refundable order
164: curl -c /tmp/trailforge-support.cookies -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"username":"support1","password":"support12345"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/auth/login
165: curl -i -b /tmp/trailforge-support.cookies -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":1.50,"reason":"authorized-check","idempotencyKey":"support-authorized-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/<ORDER_ID_FROM_SQL>/refunds
166: ```
167: 
168: Expected status codes:
169: 
170: - Unauthenticated refund request: `401`
171: - Authenticated regular user refund request: `403`
172: - Authenticated support/admin request against the refundable fixture order: `201`
173: 
174: Optional status-only checks:
175: 
176: ```bash
177: curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":0.01,"reason":"test","idempotencyKey":"unauth-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/1/refunds
178: curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -b /tmp/trailforge-user.cookies -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":0.01,"reason":"test","idempotencyKey":"user-forbidden-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/1/refunds
179: curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -b /tmp/trailforge-support.cookies -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":1.50,"reason":"authorized-check","idempotencyKey":"support-authorized-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/<ORDER_ID_FROM_SQL>/refunds
180: ```
181: 
182: ## Seeded Roles and Accounts
183: 
184: `backend/seeds/002_roles_users_seed.js` creates these default roles:
185: 
186: - `admin`
187: - `coach`
188: - `support`
189: - `user`
190: 
191: It also seeds one default account per role for local development:
192: 
193: | Role | Username | Password | Email |
194: |---|---|---|---|
195: | Admin | `admin` | `admin12345` | `admin@trailforge.local` |
196: | Coach | `coach1` | `coach12345` | `coach1@trailforge.local` |
197: | Support Agent | `support1` | `support12345` | `support1@trailforge.local` |
198: | Regular User | `athlete1` | `athlete12345` | `athlete1@trailforge.local` |
199: 
200: These are seeded local credentials only.
201: 
202: ## Project Layout
203: 
204: - `frontend/` Vue app + Dockerfile
205: - `backend/` Koa API + Dockerfile + migrations/seeds
206: 
207: ## Backend Order/Payment Foundations
208: 
209: Implemented backend APIs:
210: 
211: - `POST /api/v1/orders`
212: - `GET /api/v1/orders/:id`
213: - `GET /api/v1/orders/:id/payment-status`

219: Implemented activity APIs:

233: Implemented feed + ingestion APIs:

243: - `POST /api/v1/admin/ingestion/sources`
244: - `PATCH /api/v1/admin/ingestion/sources/:id`
245: - `POST /api/v1/admin/ingestion/scan`
246: - `GET /api/v1/admin/ingestion/logs`
247: 
248: Implemented analytics APIs (admin/support):
249: 
250: - `GET /api/v1/admin/analytics/dashboard`
251: - `GET /api/v1/admin/analytics/report`
252: - `POST /api/v1/admin/analytics/export`
253: - `GET /api/v1/admin/analytics/export-logs`
254: 
255: Analytics reports include:
256: 
257: - enrollment funnel
258: - course popularity
259: - renewal rates
260: - refund rates
261: - channel performance
262: - instructor utilization
263: - location revenue/cost
264: 
265: Sample reconciliation files:
266: 
267: - `backend/sample_data/reconciliation/recon_success_sample.csv`
268: - `backend/sample_data/reconciliation/recon_mixed_sample.csv`
269: 
270: ## Notes
271: 
272: - Analytics CSV exports are written under the backend `exports/` directory.
273: - Every export request writes an access row to `analytics_export_access_logs` including user, report type, filters JSON, row count, and output path.
274: - Feed ingestion retries are logged as immutable `retried`/`failed` events in `immutable_ingestion_logs`.
275: - Subscriber status is derived from the latest `subscription` entitlement and exposed in `GET /api/v1/auth/me`.
276: - First-time users without selected sports are routed to `/onboarding/interests` before entering the feed.

(End of file - total 276 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# TrailForge repo

This repository contains TrailForge as an offline-ready repo project with a Vue frontend, Koa backend, MySQL storage, background worker processing, feed ingestion, governance workflows, and analytics.

## Services

- `frontend` - Vue 3 + Vite role-aware application
- `backend` - Koa API with auth/RBAC, validation, auditing, domain services, analytics, and ingestion APIs
- `worker` - queue worker for payment/order sweeps and ingestion jobs
- `mysql` - MySQL 8.4 data store

## Ports

- Frontend: `5173`
- Backend API: `3000`
- MySQL: `3306`

## Primary Startup Path

```bash

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+
+set +e
+
+ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+SUMMARY_FILE="$ROOT_DIR/unit_tests/.summary"
+LOG_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/unit_tests/logs"
+
+mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
+
+TOTAL=0
+PASSED=0
+FAILED=0
+
+run_group() {
+  local name="$1"
+  local command="$2"
+  local log_file="$LOG_DIR/${name}.log"
+
+  TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
+  echo "[UNIT] Running: $name"
+  eval "$command" >"$log_file" 2>&1
+  local exit_code=$?
+
+  if [ "$exit_code" -eq 0 ]; then
+    PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
+    echo "[UNIT][PASS] $name"
+  else
+    FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
+    echo "[UNIT][FAIL] $name"
+    echo "  reason: command exited with status $exit_code"
+    echo "  log snippet:"
+    tail -n 25 "$log_file"
+  fi
+}
+
+run_group "backend_unit_suite" "npm --prefix backend test"
+run_group "frontend_unit_suite" "npm --prefix frontend test"
+
+echo ""
+echo "UNIT TEST SUMMARY"
+echo "TOTAL=$TOTAL"
+echo "PASSED=$PASSED"
+echo "FAILED=$FAILED"
+
+cat >"$SUMMARY_FILE" <<EOF
+TOTAL=$TOTAL
+PASSED=$PASSED
+FAILED=$FAILED
+EOF
+
+if [ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ]; then
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+exit 0
*** End Patch"}

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set +e

ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
SUMMARY_FILE="$ROOT_DIR/unit_tests/.summary"
LOG_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/unit_tests/logs"

mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"

TOTAL=0
PASSED=0
FAILED=0

run_group() {
  local name="$1"
  local command="$2"
  local log_file="$LOG_DIR/${name}.log"

  TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
  echo "[UNIT] Running: $name"
  eval "$command" >"$log_file" 2>&1
  local exit_code=$?

  if [ "$exit_code" -eq 0 ]; then
    PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
    echo "[UNIT][PASS] $name"
  else
    FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
    echo "[UNIT][FAIL] $name"
    echo "  reason: command exited with status $exit_code"
    echo "  log snippet:"
    tail -n 25 "$log_file"
  fi
}

run_group "backend_unit_suite" "npm --prefix backend test"
run_group "frontend_unit_suite" "npm --prefix frontend test"

echo ""
echo "UNIT TEST SUMMARY"
echo "TOTAL=$TOTAL"
echo "PASSED=$PASSED"
echo "FAILED=$FAILED"

cat >"$SUMMARY_FILE" <<EOF
TOTAL=$TOTAL
PASSED=$PASSED
FAILED=$FAILED
EOF

if [ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ]; then
  exit 1
fi

exit 0

+PASSED=$((UNIT_PASSED + API_PASSED))
+FAILED=$((UNIT_FAILED + API_FAILED))
+
+echo ""
+echo "===== CONSOLIDATED SUMMARY ====="
+echo "TOTAL=$TOTAL"
+echo "PASSED=$PASSED"
+echo "FAILED=$FAILED"
+
+if [ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ] || [ "$UNIT_EXIT" -ne 0 ] || [ "$API_EXIT" -ne 0 ]; then
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set +e

ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
UNIT_SUMMARY="$ROOT_DIR/unit_tests/.summary"
API_SUMMARY="$ROOT_DIR/API_tests/.summary"

mkdir -p "$ROOT_DIR/unit_tests" "$ROOT_DIR/API_tests"

if [ ! -d "$ROOT_DIR/backend/node_modules" ]; then
  echo "Installing backend dependencies..."
  npm --prefix "$ROOT_DIR/backend" install
fi

if [ ! -d "$ROOT_DIR/frontend/node_modules" ]; then
  echo "Installing frontend dependencies..."
  npm --prefix "$ROOT_DIR/frontend" install
fi

echo ""
echo "===== RUNNING UNIT TESTS ====="
bash "$ROOT_DIR/unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh"
UNIT_EXIT=$?

echo ""
echo "===== RUNNING API TESTS ====="
bash "$ROOT_DIR/API_tests/run_api_tests.sh"
API_EXIT=$?

UNIT_TOTAL=0
UNIT_PASSED=0
UNIT_FAILED=0
API_TOTAL=0
API_PASSED=0
API_FAILED=0

if [ -f "$UNIT_SUMMARY" ]; then
  # shellcheck disable=SC1090
  source "$UNIT_SUMMARY"
  UNIT_TOTAL=${TOTAL:-0}
  UNIT_PASSED=${PASSED:-0}
  UNIT_FAILED=${FAILED:-0}
fi

if [ -f "$API_SUMMARY" ]; then
  # shellcheck disable=SC1090
  source "$API_SUMMARY"
  API_TOTAL=${TOTAL:-0}
  API_PASSED=${PASSED:-0}
  API_FAILED=${FAILED:-0}
fi

TOTAL=$((UNIT_TOTAL + API_TOTAL))
PASSED=$((UNIT_PASSED + API_PASSED))
FAILED=$((UNIT_FAILED + API_FAILED))

echo ""
echo "===== CONSOLIDATED SUMMARY ====="
echo "TOTAL=$TOTAL"
echo "PASSED=$PASSED"
echo "FAILED=$FAILED"

if [ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ] || [ "$UNIT_EXIT" -ne 0 ] || [ "$API_EXIT" -ne 0 ]; then
  exit 1
fi

exit 0

files.before:
# TrailForge repo

This repository contains TrailForge as an offline-ready repo project with a Vue frontend, Koa backend, MySQL storage, background worker processing, feed ingestion, governance workflows, and analytics.

## Services

- `frontend` - Vue 3 + Vite role-aware application
- `backend` - Koa API with auth/RBAC, validation, auditing, domain services, analytics, and ingestion APIs
- `worker` - queue worker for payment/order sweeps and ingestion jobs
- `mysql` - MySQL 8.4 data store

## Ports

- Frontend: `5173`
- Backend API: `3000`
- MySQL: `3306`

## Primary Startup Path

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

This is the default and recommended startup flow.

When started from root compose, backend automatically applies migrations and seeds before serving traffic. No hidden manual initialization steps are required for a clean local boot.

## Quick Demo Credentials (Seeded)

From `backend/seeds/002_roles_users_seed.js`:

| Role | Username | Password | Email |
|---|---|---|---|
| Admin | `admin` | `admin12345` | `admin@trailforge.local` |
| Coach | `coach1` | `coach12345` | `coach1@trailforge.local` |
| Support Agent | `support1` | `support12345` | `support1@trailforge.local` |
| Regular User | `athlete1` | `athlete12345` | `athlete1@trailforge.local` |


## Optional Local Scripts

```bash
npm run up
npm run down
npm run logs
npm run migrate
npm run migrate:down
npm run seed
npm run test
npm run test:integration:db
```

## Verification Steps

1. Start stack:

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

2. Verify backend health:

```bash
curl http://localhost:3000/health
```

Expected response includes `success: true` and `data.status: "ok"`.

3. Verify auth foundation:

```bash
curl -c /tmp/trailforge-session.cookies -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"username":"admin","password":"admin12345"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/auth/login
curl -b /tmp/trailforge-session.cookies http://localhost:3000/api/v1/auth/me
curl -b /tmp/trailforge-session.cookies http://localhost:3000/api/v1/admin/test
```

Expected behavior:

- Login returns a signed session cookie.
- `/api/v1/auth/me` returns current user data.
- `/api/v1/admin/test` is allowed for admin role and denied for non-admin users.

4. Verify frontend app shell:

- Open `http://localhost:5173`
- Confirm TrailForge app shell renders and API base URL is shown.

5. Verify database connectivity:

- Check backend logs for successful MySQL check at startup.

6. Run full test pass:

```bash
npm run test
```

## Backend Verification Workflows

### Default fast/local backend tests (no DB required)

```bash
npm --prefix backend install
npm --prefix backend test
```

Expected outcome:

- Vitest completes successfully in local environments even when MySQL is unavailable.
- Refund authorization regression coverage is still exercised (`401`, `403`, privileged success path).

### DB-backed refund integration tests (opt-in)

Prerequisites:

- MySQL is running and reachable by backend env vars.
- Schema migrations and seeds are already applied.

Run integration coverage explicitly:

```bash
npm --prefix backend run test:integration:db
```

Expected outcome:

- `backend/tests/refund-persistence.integration.test.js` runs (not skipped).
- Authorized refund request persists side effects end-to-end:
  - refund row created,
  - order refunded amount and status updated,
  - payment status updated,
  - ledger refund entry created.

If MySQL is unavailable, this command fails fast with a prerequisite message instead of running the baseline suite.

### Docker checklist (full stack)

1. Start services:

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

2. Health check:

```bash
curl http://localhost:3000/health
```

3. Refund authorization checks:

```bash
# Unauthenticated should be 401
curl -i -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":0.01,"reason":"test","idempotencyKey":"unauth-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/1/refunds

# Authenticated regular user should be 403
curl -c /tmp/trailforge-user.cookies -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"username":"athlete1","password":"athlete12345"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/auth/login
curl -i -b /tmp/trailforge-user.cookies -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":0.01,"reason":"test","idempotencyKey":"user-forbidden-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/1/refunds

# Create a refundable fixture order/payment in MySQL (returns new order id)
docker exec trailforge-mysql mysql -utrailforge -ptrailforge -D trailforge -Nse "SET @uid=(SELECT id FROM users WHERE username='athlete1' LIMIT 1); SET @pid=(SELECT id FROM users WHERE username='support1' LIMIT 1); INSERT INTO courses_services (kind,title,description,provider_user_id,status) VALUES ('service', CONCAT('Docker Refund Fixture ', UNIX_TIMESTAMP()), 'docker refund verification fixture', @pid, 'active'); SET @csid=LAST_INSERT_ID(); INSERT INTO orders (user_id,course_service_id,order_type,order_status,total_amount_cents,paid_amount_cents,refunded_amount_cents,currency,idempotency_key) VALUES (@uid,@csid,'service','paid',1000,1000,0,'USD', CONCAT('docker-refund-order-', UNIX_TIMESTAMP())); SET @oid=LAST_INSERT_ID(); INSERT INTO payments (order_id,provider,provider_txn_id,payment_status,amount_cents,signature_valid,raw_payload,confirmed_at) VALUES (@oid,'wechat_pay', CONCAT('docker-txn-', UNIX_TIMESTAMP()),'confirmed',1000,1,JSON_OBJECT('source','docker-refund-check'),CURRENT_TIMESTAMP); SELECT @oid;"

# Authenticated support/admin should succeed on that refundable order
curl -c /tmp/trailforge-support.cookies -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"username":"support1","password":"support12345"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/auth/login
curl -i -b /tmp/trailforge-support.cookies -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":1.50,"reason":"authorized-check","idempotencyKey":"support-authorized-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/<ORDER_ID_FROM_SQL>/refunds
```

Expected status codes:

- Unauthenticated refund request: `401`
- Authenticated regular user refund request: `403`
- Authenticated support/admin request against the refundable fixture order: `201`

Optional status-only checks:

```bash
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":0.01,"reason":"test","idempotencyKey":"unauth-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/1/refunds
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -b /tmp/trailforge-user.cookies -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":0.01,"reason":"test","idempotencyKey":"user-forbidden-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/1/refunds
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -b /tmp/trailforge-support.cookies -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":1.50,"reason":"authorized-check","idempotencyKey":"support-authorized-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/<ORDER_ID_FROM_SQL>/refunds
```

## Seeded Roles and Accounts

`backend/seeds/002_roles_users_seed.js` creates these default roles:

- `admin`
- `coach`
- `support`
- `user`

It also seeds one default account per role for local development:

| Role | Username | Password | Email |
|---|---|---|---|
| Admin | `admin` | `admin12345` | `admin@trailforge.local` |
| Coach | `coach1` | `coach12345` | `coach1@trailforge.local` |
| Support Agent | `support1` | `support12345` | `support1@trailforge.local` |
| Regular User | `athlete1` | `athlete12345` | `athlete1@trailforge.local` |

These are seeded local credentials only.

## Project Layout

- `frontend/` Vue app + Dockerfile
- `backend/` Koa API + Dockerfile + migrations/seeds

## Backend Order/Payment Foundations

Implemented backend APIs:

- `POST /api/v1/orders`
- `GET /api/v1/orders/:id`
- `GET /api/v1/orders/:id/payment-status`
- `POST /api/v1/payments/imports` (admin/support)
- `GET /api/v1/payments/imports/:importId` (admin/support)
- `POST /api/v1/payments/orders/:id/refunds`
- `POST /api/v1/admin/jobs/process-once` (admin, test helper)

Implemented activity APIs:

- `GET /api/v1/places`
- `POST /api/v1/places`
- `PATCH /api/v1/places/:placeId`
- `DELETE /api/v1/places/:placeId`
- `GET /api/v1/activities`
- `POST /api/v1/activities`
- `GET /api/v1/activities/:activityId`
- `PATCH /api/v1/activities/:activityId`
- `DELETE /api/v1/activities/:activityId`
- `POST /api/v1/activities/:activityId/gpx`
- `GET /api/v1/activities/:activityId/coordinates`

Implemented feed + ingestion APIs:

- `GET /api/v1/feed`
- `POST /api/v1/feed/actions`
- `GET /api/v1/feed/preferences`
- `PUT /api/v1/feed/preferences`
- `GET /api/v1/follows/mine`
- `POST /api/v1/follows/:userId`
- `DELETE /api/v1/follows/:userId`
- `GET /api/v1/admin/ingestion/sources`
- `POST /api/v1/admin/ingestion/sources`
- `PATCH /api/v1/admin/ingestion/sources/:id`
- `POST /api/v1/admin/ingestion/scan`
- `GET /api/v1/admin/ingestion/logs`

Implemented analytics APIs (admin/support):

- `GET /api/v1/admin/analytics/dashboard`
- `GET /api/v1/admin/analytics/report`
- `POST /api/v1/admin/analytics/export`
- `GET /api/v1/admin/analytics/export-logs`

Analytics reports include:

- enrollment funnel
- course popularity
- renewal rates
- refund rates
- channel performance
- instructor utilization
- location revenue/cost

Sample reconciliation files:

- `backend/sample_data/reconciliation/recon_success_sample.csv`
- `backend/sample_data/reconciliation/recon_mixed_sample.csv`

## Notes

- Analytics CSV exports are written under the backend `exports/` directory.
- Every export request writes an access row to `analytics_export_access_logs` including user, report type, filters JSON, row count, and output path.
- Feed ingestion retries are logged as immutable `retried`/`failed` events in `immutable_ingestion_logs`.
- Subscriber status is derived from the latest `subscription` entitlement and exposed in `GET /api/v1/auth/me`.
- First-time users without selected sports are routed to `/onboarding/interests` before entering the feed.

curl -c /tmp/trailforge-support.cookies -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"username":"support1","password":"support12345"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/auth/login
curl -i -b /tmp/trailforge-support.cookies -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":1.50,"reason":"authorized-check","idempotencyKey":"support-authorized-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/<ORDER_ID_FROM_SQL>/refunds
```

Expected status codes:

- Unauthenticated refund request: `401`
- Authenticated regular user refund request: `403`
- Authenticated support/admin request against the refundable fixture order: `201`

Optional status-only checks:

```bash
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":0.01,"reason":"test","idempotencyKey":"unauth-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/1/refunds
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -b /tmp/trailforge-user.cookies -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":0.01,"reason":"test","idempotencyKey":"user-forbidden-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/1/refunds
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -b /tmp/trailforge-support.cookies -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"amountDollars":1.50,"reason":"authorized-check","idempotencyKey":"support-authorized-check"}' http://localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/orders/<ORDER_ID_FROM_SQL>/refunds
```

## Seeded Roles and Accounts

`backend/seeds/002_roles_users_seed.js` creates these default roles:

- `admin`
- `coach`
- `support`
- `user`

It also seeds one default account per role for local development:

| Role | Username | Password | Email |
|---|---|---|---|
| Admin | `admin` | `admin12345` | `admin@trailforge.local` |
| Coach | `coach1` | `coach12345` | `coach1@trailforge.local` |
| Support Agent | `support1` | `support12345` | `support1@trailforge.local` |
| Regular User | `athlete1` | `athlete12345` | `athlete1@trailforge.local` |

These are seeded local credentials only.

## Unified Acceptance Test Runner

Project root includes required acceptance test directories and one-click runner:

- `unit_tests/` - unit suite wrapper (backend + frontend)
- `API_tests/` - API functional tests for auth, follows, refunds, and staff-review permissions
- `run_tests.sh` - unified runner with consolidated summary and exit code

### Prerequisites

- Node.js + npm available in shell
- Dependencies installed (`backend/node_modules`, `frontend/node_modules`)
  - `run_tests.sh` auto-installs missing dependencies

### One-click command

```bash
./run_tests.sh
```

### Output format

- Per test/group output includes:
  - test name
  - status (`PASS`/`FAIL`)
  - failure reason (for failed tests)
  - log snippet (for failed tests)
- Final strict summary block:

```text
TOTAL=<number>
PASSED=<number>
FAILED=<number>
```

### Exit codes

- `0` - all suites passed
- `1` - one or more tests failed

### Coverage notes

- API tests cover normal and abnormal scenarios, including 400/401/403/404/409 paths where applicable.
- API tests include pre/post validation for state-changing follow APIs.
- If some business domains are not present in this repository (for example dedicated hiring/inventory modules), `API_tests` summary prints explicit `TODO_GAP` markers.

## Project Layout

- `frontend/` Vue app + Dockerfile
- `backend/` Koa API + Dockerfile + migrations/seeds

## Backend Order/Payment Foundations

Implemented backend APIs:

- `POST /api/v1/orders`
- `GET /api/v1/orders/:id`
- `GET /api/v1/orders/:id/payment-status`
- `POST /api/v1/payments/imports` (admin/support)
- `GET /api/v1/payments/imports/:importId` (admin/support)
- `POST /api/v1/payments/orders/:id/refunds`
- `POST /api/v1/admin/jobs/process-once` (admin, test helper)

Implemented activity APIs:

- `GET /api/v1/places`
- `POST /api/v1/places`
- `PATCH /api/v1/places/:placeId`
- `DELETE /api/v1/places/:placeId`
- `GET /api/v1/activities`
- `POST /api/v1/activities`
- `GET /api/v1/activities/:activityId`
- `PATCH /api/v1/activities/:activityId`
- `DELETE /api/v1/activities/:activityId`
- `POST /api/v1/activities/:activityId/gpx`
- `GET /api/v1/activities/:activityId/coordinates`

Implemented feed + ingestion APIs:

- `GET /api/v1/feed`
- `POST /api/v1/feed/actions`
- `GET /api/v1/feed/preferences`
- `PUT /api/v1/feed/preferences`
- `GET /api/v1/follows/mine`
- `POST /api/v1/follows/:userId`
- `DELETE /api/v1/follows/:userId`
- `GET /api/v1/admin/ingestion/sources`
- `POST /api/v1/admin/ingestion/sources`
- `PATCH /api/v1/admin/ingestion/sources/:id`
- `POST /api/v1/admin/ingestion/scan`
- `GET /api/v1/admin/ingestion/logs`

Implemented analytics APIs (admin/support):

- `GET /api/v1/admin/analytics/dashboard`
- `GET /api/v1/admin/analytics/report`
- `POST /api/v1/admin/analytics/export`
- `GET /api/v1/admin/analytics/export-logs`

Analytics reports include:

- enrollment funnel
- course popularity
- renewal rates
- refund rates
- channel performance
- instructor utilization
- location revenue/cost

Sample reconciliation files:

- `backend/sample_data/reconciliation/recon_success_sample.csv`
- `backend/sample_data/reconciliation/recon_mixed_sample.csv`

## Notes

- Analytics CSV exports are written under the backend `exports/` directory.
- Every export request writes an access row to `analytics_export_access_logs` including user, report type, filters JSON, row count, and output path.
- Feed ingestion retries are logged as immutable `retried`/`failed` events in `immutable_ingestion_logs`.
- Subscriber status is derived from the latest `subscription` entitlement and exposed in `GET /api/v1/auth/me`.
- First-time users without selected sports are routed to `/onboarding/interests` before entering the feed.

content.text:
Implemented. I added a full runnable/repeatable acceptance test framework at project root with mandatory structure, one-click execution, detailed per-test output, and strict summary/exit behavior.

**Created / Updated Files**
- Created `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh`
- Created `API_tests/run_api_tests.sh`
- Created `API_tests/run_api_tests.js`
- Created `run_tests.sh`
- Updated `README.md`
- (Generated during runs) `unit_tests/logs/*`, `unit_tests/.summary`, `API_tests/.summary`

**What Each Suite Covers**
- `unit_tests/` (via `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh`)
  - Runs backend unit suite: `npm --prefix backend test`
  - Runs frontend unit suite: `npm --prefix frontend test`
  - Reports PASS/FAIL per logical group (backend/frontend), includes failure reason + log snippet on failure
- `API_tests/` (via `API_tests/run_api_tests.js`)
  - Auth/login:
    - happy path
    - missing required parameter (400)
    - invalid parameter type/format (400)
  - Permission-sensitive refunds API:
    - unauthorized (401)
    - forbidden role (403)
    - privileged happy path (201)
    - not found propagation (404)
  - Follows API:
    - state-changing pre/post validation (GET pre, POST mutate, GET post, DELETE, GET post)
    - invalid path parameter format (400)
  - Staff review appeals API:
    - unauthorized (401)
    - forbidden role (403)
    - support happy path (200)
  - Reviews create conflict:
    - conflict path (409)

**Sample Final Summary Output Format**
```text
===== CONSOLIDATED SUMMARY =====
TOTAL=15
PASSED=15
FAILED=0
```

API suite additionally prints explicit coverage-gap TODO markers when applicable:
```text
TODO_GAP: dedicated hiring module endpoints are not present in this repository
TODO_GAP: dedicated inventory module endpoints are not present in this repository
TODO_GAP: compliance module is represented via reviews governance routes only; standalone compliance API not found
```

**Known Limitations / Gaps**
- Dedicated `hiring` and `inventory` modules are not present in this repository, so those API paths cannot be directly covered.
- ΓÇ£ComplianceΓÇ¥ appears embedded in review-governance flows rather than a standalone module; this is explicitly flagged in runner output with `TODO_GAP`.
- API tests are deterministic and repeatable by design (mocked boundary dependencies), but they validate API behavior/contracts rather than full DB integration semantics.

**Exact Command to Run Acceptance Tests**
- From project root (`repo/`):
```bash
./run_tests.sh
```

Use this prompt:


You are working in the `repo` repository root only.  
Goal: fix all issues identified in the latest self-test report, with changes limited to this repo.
## Critical issues to fix
1) Backend security bug (highest priority)
- Fix object-level authorization for `GET /api/v1/reviews/:id`.
- Ensure only:
  - the review owner, or
  - privileged roles (`admin`, `support`, `coach`)
  can access review details.
- Return `403 FORBIDDEN` for authenticated but unauthorized users.
- Keep existing behavior for authorized users and arbitration visibility rules.
2) Missing backend test coverage for the security bug
- Add API/service tests that prove:
  - owner can access own review detail,
  - privileged roles can access review detail,
  - non-owner regular user gets `403`,
  - non-authenticated user still gets `401`.
- Ensure tests are runnable with existing backend test commands.
3) Frontend verification doc mismatch
- `frontend/README.md` references `verification.md` but it is missing.
- Add `frontend/verification.md` with accurate, reproducible verification steps that match actual scripts and current app behavior.
- Keep instructions local to this repo and avoid Docker-only dependency for basic checks where possible.
4) Frontend E2E gap
- Add a minimal E2E setup and at least one meaningful E2E test covering a critical journey:
  - auth/route guard behavior and one core flow (e.g., feed action or review/order path).
- Integrate E2E command(s) into frontend docs.
## Constraints
- Do not modify anything outside `repo`.
- Do not use Docker commands in your execution.
- Do not change unrelated business logic.
- Preserve existing API response shape unless required for the authorization fix.
- Keep changes minimal, focused, and production-safe.
## Required output
1) Implement code + tests + docs changes.
2) Run and report results for:
   - backend tests,
   - frontend tests,
   - frontend build,
   - new E2E test command (if added).
3) Provide a concise changelog:
   - files changed,
   - why each change was made,
   - how each issue from the report is now resolved.

<path>C:\Users\dagim\Projects\Eagle Point AI\Task-42\repo\backend\src\modules\reviews\reviews.read.service.js</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: const { pool } = require("../../db/pool");
2: const ApiError = require("../../errors/api-error");
3: const { canAppeal } = require("./review.rules");
4: 
5: function toReviewImagePreviews(imageRows) {
6:   const imagesByReview = new Map();
7:   for (const image of imageRows) {
8:     if (!imagesByReview.has(image.review_id)) {
9:       imagesByReview.set(image.review_id, []);
10:     }
11:     imagesByReview.get(image.review_id).push({
12:       id: image.id,
13:       mimeType: image.mime_type,
14:       url: `/api/v1/reviews/images/${image.id}`
15:     });
16:   }
17:   return imagesByReview;
18: }
19: 
20: function toFollowupsByReview(followupRows) {
21:   const followupByReview = new Map();
22:   for (const followup of followupRows) {
23:     followupByReview.set(followup.review_id, {
24:       id: followup.id,
25:       followupText: followup.followup_text,
26:       createdAt: followup.created_at
27:     });
28:   }
29:   return followupByReview;
30: }
31: 
32: async function getReviewDetail({ reviewId, requester }) {
33:   const [reviewRows] = await pool.query(
34:     `
35:       SELECT r.*, u.username, p.display_name
36:       FROM reviews r
37:       JOIN users u ON u.id = r.user_id
38:       LEFT JOIN user_profiles p ON p.user_id = r.user_id
39:       WHERE r.id = ?
40:       LIMIT 1
41:     `,
42:     [reviewId]
43:   );
44:   if (!reviewRows.length) {
45:     throw new ApiError(404, "REVIEW_NOT_FOUND", "Review not found");
46:   }
47: 
48:   const review = reviewRows[0];
49:   const hidden = review.review_state === "under_arbitration";
50:   const displayName = review.anonymous_display ? "Anonymous User" : review.display_name || review.username;
51: 
52:   const [dimensionRows] = await pool.query(
53:     `
54:       SELECT ds.dimension_config_id, ds.score, dc.label, dc.key_name
55:       FROM review_dimension_scores ds
56:       JOIN review_dimension_configs dc ON dc.id = ds.dimension_config_id
57:       WHERE ds.review_id = ?
58:     `,
59:     [reviewId]
60:   );
61:   const [imageRows] = await pool.query(
62:     "SELECT id, mime_type, file_size_bytes, sort_order FROM review_images WHERE review_id = ? ORDER BY sort_order ASC, id ASC",
63:     [reviewId]
64:   );
65:   const [followupRows] = await pool.query("SELECT * FROM review_followups WHERE review_id = ? LIMIT 1", [reviewId]);
66:   const [replyRows] = await pool.query(
67:     `
68:       SELECT rr.id, rr.parent_reply_id, rr.reply_text, rr.created_at, rr.author_role, u.username
69:       FROM review_replies rr
70:       JOIN users u ON u.id = rr.author_user_id
71:       WHERE rr.review_id = ?
72:       ORDER BY rr.id ASC
73:     `,
74:     [reviewId]
75:   );
76:   const [appealRows] = await pool.query("SELECT * FROM appeals WHERE review_id = ? ORDER BY id DESC", [reviewId]);
77:   const appeal = appealRows[0] || null;
78: 
79:   let timeline = [];
80:   if (appeal) {
81:     const [timelineRows] = await pool.query(
82:       "SELECT event_type, event_status, event_note, created_at FROM appeal_timeline_events WHERE appeal_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC",
83:       [appeal.id]
84:     );
85:     timeline = timelineRows;
86:   }
87: 
88:   const canAppealNow = requester && requester.id === review.user_id && canAppeal(review.published_at) && (!appeal || appeal.appeal_status === "resolved");
89: 
90:   return {
91:     id: review.id,
92:     orderId: review.order_id,
93:     userId: review.user_id,
94:     rating: review.rating,
95:     reviewState: review.review_state,
96:     displayName,
97:     anonymousDisplay: Boolean(review.anonymous_display),
98:     reviewText: hidden ? "Content hidden during arbitration" : review.review_text,
99:     publishedAt: review.published_at,
100:     dimensions: dimensionRows,
101:     images: hidden ? [] : imageRows,
102:     followup: hidden ? null : followupRows[0] || null,
103:     replies: replyRows,
104:     appeal: appeal
105:       ? {
106:           id: appeal.id,
107:           status: appeal.appeal_status,
108:           submittedAt: appeal.submitted_at,
109:           resolvedAt: appeal.resolved_at,
110:           timeline
111:         }
112:       : null,
113:     canAppeal: Boolean(canAppealNow)
114:   };
115: }
116: 
117: async function listUserReviews(userId) {
118:   const [rows] = await pool.query(
119:     `
120:       SELECT r.id, r.order_id, r.rating, r.review_state, r.anonymous_display, r.review_text, r.published_at, r.created_at,
121:              o.order_status
122:       FROM reviews r
123:       JOIN orders o ON o.id = r.order_id
124:       WHERE r.user_id = ?
125:       ORDER BY r.id DESC
126:     `,
127:     [userId]
128:   );
129: 
130:   if (!rows.length) {
131:     return [];
132:   }
133: 
134:   const reviewIds = rows.map((row) => row.id);
135:   const [imageRows] = await pool.query(
136:     `
137:       SELECT review_id, id, mime_type, sort_order
138:       FROM review_images
139:       WHERE review_id IN (?)
140:       ORDER BY review_id ASC, sort_order ASC, id ASC
141:     `,
142:     [reviewIds]
143:   );
144:   const [followupRows] = await pool.query(
145:     `
146:       SELECT id, review_id, followup_text, created_at
147:       FROM review_followups
148:       WHERE review_id IN (?)
149:     `,
150:     [reviewIds]
151:   );
152: 
153:   const imagesByReview = toReviewImagePreviews(imageRows);
154:   const followupByReview = toFollowupsByReview(followupRows);
155: 
156:   return rows.map((row) => {
157:     const hidden = row.review_state === "under_arbitration";
158:     const images = hidden ? [] : imagesByReview.get(row.id) || [];
159:     return {
160:       ...row,
161:       image_count: images.length,
162:       image_previews: images.slice(0, 3),
163:       followup: hidden ? null : followupByReview.get(row.id) || null
164:     };
165:   });
166: }
167: 
168: module.exports = {
169:   getReviewDetail,
170:   listUserReviews,
171:   toReviewImagePreviews,
172:   toFollowupsByReview
173: };

<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Frontend (TrailForge)
2: 
3: This frontend is a Vue 3 + Vite app with route-level auth/RBAC, business flows (feed, reviews, orders, activities), and offline-friendly behavior.
4: 
5: ## Commands
6: 
7: ```bash
8: npm install
9: npm run dev
10: npm run test
11: npm run build
12: npm run preview
13: ```
14: 
15: ## Test Strategy
16: 
17: - Unit/API tests validate shared request behavior (including 401/403 error propagation).
18: - Route guard tests validate auth/guest/RBAC navigation policy in `src/router.js`.
19: - Page integration tests cover critical success and failure paths:
20:   - feed optimistic action + rollback + duplicate-click protection,
21:   - review create + image validation edges + follow-up/appeal flow protections,
22:   - orders refund/complete success and error handling.
23: - Existing app-shell test remains as a baseline smoke check.
24: 
25: ## Offline Strategy
26: 
27: Offline behavior is intentionally simple and bounded:
28: 
29: 1. **Service Worker (`public/sw.js`)**
30:    - App shell cache for offline reload (`/`, `/index.html`).
31:    - Runtime cache for same-origin static assets.
32:    - Authenticated/private API responses are not cached in the service worker.
33: 
34: 2. **Client persistence (`src/offline/persistence.js`)**
35:    - Stores last feed snapshot and feed preferences in `localStorage`.
36:    - Feed page falls back to cached data when network requests fail.
37:    - Cached-mode indicator is shown in the feed UI.
38: 
39: 3. **Offline mutation UX (`src/offline/mutation-intents.js`)**
40:    - Failed offline mutation attempts create local retry intents.
41:    - UI shows pending retry count and clear action.
42:    - User gets explicit retry messaging (no silent failure).
43: 
44: 4. **Account-switch isolation (`src/offline/private-data.js`)**
45:    - Logout and user/session transitions clear persisted private offline state.
46:    - Service worker receives a private-cache clear message to purge legacy private API cache buckets.
47: 
48: ## Verification Boundaries
49: 
50: - Backend authorization/business rules are still backend-owned and must be verified against a running backend.
51: - Offline mutation intents are not auto-replayed; users retry manually after reconnecting.
52: - Binary upload durability for offline review image uploads is not persisted across full browser restarts.
53: - E2E tests mock API responses at browser level; backend data integrity remains backend-suite responsibility.
54: 
55: See `verification.md` for reproducible acceptance checks.

(End of file - total 55 lines)
</content>

# Frontend (TrailForge)

This frontend is a Vue 3 + Vite app with route-level auth/RBAC, business flows (feed, reviews, orders, activities), and offline-friendly behavior.

## Commands

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
npm run test
npm run build
npm run preview
```

## Test Strategy

- Unit/API tests validate shared request behavior (including 401/403 error propagation).
- Route guard tests validate auth/guest/RBAC navigation policy in `src/router.js`.
- Page integration tests cover critical success and failure paths:
  - feed optimistic action + rollback + duplicate-click protection,

26:   { path: "/orders", name: "orders", component: OrdersPage, meta: { auth: true, roles: ["user", "coach", "support", "admin"] } },

32:   { path: "/onboarding/interests", name: "onboarding-interests", component: OnboardingInterestsPage, meta: { auth: true, roles: ["user", "coach", "support", "admin"] } },

37:   { path: "/:pathMatch(.*)*", name: "not-found", component: NotFoundPage }
38: ];

40: export function registerRouteGuards(router, options = {}) {
41:   const resolveSession = options.resolveSession || useSession;
42:   const loadFeedPreferences = options.loadFeedPreferences || getFeedPreferences;
43: 
44:   router.beforeEach(async (to) => {
45:     const { state, bootstrapSession, hasRole } = resolveSession();
46:     if (!state.initialized) {
47:       await bootstrapSession();
48:     }
49: 
50:     if (to.meta.guestOnly && state.user) {
51:       return { name: "feed" };
52:     }
53: 
54:     if (to.meta.auth && !state.user) {
55:       return { name: "login" };
56:     }
57: 
58:     if (to.meta.roles && state.user && !hasRole(to.meta.roles)) {
59:       return { name: "feed" };
60:     }
61: 
62:     if (to.name === "feed" && state.user) {
63:       try {
64:         const prefs = await loadFeedPreferences();
65:         const sports = prefs.data?.preferredSports || [];
66:         if (!sports.length) {
67:           return { name: "onboarding-interests" };
68:         }
69:       } catch {
70:         return true;
71:       }
72:     }
73: 
74:     return true;
75:   });
76: }
77: 
78: export function createAppRouter(history = createWebHistory(), guardOptions) {
79:   const router = createRouter({
80:     history,
81:     routes
82:   });
83:   registerRouteGuards(router, guardOptions);
84:   return router;
85: }
86: 
87: const router = createAppRouter();
88: 
89: export default router;

<content>1: import { reactive, readonly } from "vue";
2: import { getCurrentUser, login as apiLogin } from "./api";
3: 
4: const state = reactive({
5:   initialized: false,
6:   loading: false,
7:   user: null,
8:   error: ""
9: });
10: 
11: function hasRole(requiredRoles) {
12:   if (!state.user) {
13:     return false;
14:   }
15:   const roles = state.user.roles || [];
16:   return requiredRoles.some((role) => roles.includes(role));
17: }
18: 
19: async function bootstrapSession() {
20:   state.loading = true;
21:   state.error = "";
22:   try {
23:     const response = await getCurrentUser();
24:     state.user = response.data;
25:   } catch {
26:     state.user = null;
27:   } finally {
28:     state.loading = false;
29:     state.initialized = true;
30:   }
31: }
32: 
33: async function login(credentials) {
34:   state.loading = true;
35:   state.error = "";
36:   try {
37:     await apiLogin(credentials);
38:     const me = await getCurrentUser();
39:     state.user = me.data;
40:     return state.user;
41:   } catch (error) {
42:     state.error = error.message || "Login failed";
43:     throw error;
44:   } finally {
45:     state.loading = false;
46:   }
47: }
48: 
49: async function refreshUser() {
50:   try {
51:     const me = await getCurrentUser();
52:     state.user = me.data;
53:   } catch {
54:     state.user = null;
55:   }
56: }
57: 
58: function clearSession() {
59:   state.user = null;
60: }
61: 
62: export function useSession() {
63:   return {
64:     state: readonly(state),
65:     hasRole,
66:     bootstrapSession,
67:     login,
68:     refreshUser,
69:     clearSession
70:   };
71: }

<path>C:\Users\dagim\Projects\Eagle Point AI\Task-42\repo\backend\src\middleware\auth.js</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: const { pool } = require("../db/pool");
2: const ApiError = require("../errors/api-error");
3: const { hashSessionToken } = require("../security/session");
4: 
5: const SESSION_COOKIE = "trailforge_session";
6: 
7: async function optionalAuth(ctx, next) {
8:   const token = ctx.cookies.get(SESSION_COOKIE, { signed: true });
9:   if (!token) {
10:     await next();
11:     return;
12:   }
13: 
14:   const tokenHash = hashSessionToken(token);
15:   const [rows] = await pool.query(
16:     `
17:       SELECT s.id AS session_id, s.user_id, s.expires_at, u.username, u.email, u.status
18:       FROM auth_sessions s
19:       JOIN users u ON u.id = s.user_id
20:       WHERE s.session_token_hash = ?
21:         AND s.status = 'active'
22:         AND s.expires_at > CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
23:       LIMIT 1
24:     `,
25:     [tokenHash]
26:   );
27: 
28:   if (!rows.length) {
29:     ctx.cookies.set(SESSION_COOKIE, "", { signed: true, maxAge: 0 });
30:     await next();
31:     return;
32:   }
33: 
34:   const [roleRows] = await pool.query(
35:     `
36:       SELECT r.code
37:       FROM user_roles ur
38:       JOIN roles r ON r.id = ur.role_id
39:       WHERE ur.user_id = ?
40:     `,
41:     [rows[0].user_id]
42:   );
43: 
44:   ctx.state.user = {
45:     id: rows[0].user_id,
46:     username: rows[0].username,
47:     email: rows[0].email,
48:     status: rows[0].status,
49:     sessionId: rows[0].session_id,
50:     roles: roleRows.map((row) => row.code)
51:   };
52: 
53:   await pool.query("UPDATE auth_sessions SET last_seen_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?", [rows[0].session_id]);
54: 
55:   await next();
56: }
57: 
58: async function requireAuth(ctx, next) {
59:   if (!ctx.state.user) {
60:     throw new ApiError(401, "UNAUTHORIZED", "Authentication required");
61:   }
62: 
63:   if (ctx.state.user.status !== "active") {
64:     throw new ApiError(403, "ACCOUNT_DISABLED", "Account is not active");
65:   }
66: 
67:   await next();
68: }
69: 
70: function requireRole(allowedRoles) {
71:   return async (ctx, next) => {
72:     if (!ctx.state.user) {
73:       throw new ApiError(401, "UNAUTHORIZED", "Authentication required");
74:     }
75: 
76:     const hasRole = ctx.state.user.roles.some((role) => allowedRoles.includes(role));
77:     if (!hasRole) {
78:       throw new ApiError(403, "FORBIDDEN", "Insufficient role permissions");
79:     }
80: 
81:     await next();
82:   };
83: }
84: 
85: module.exports = {
86:   SESSION_COOKIE,
87:   optionalAuth,
88:   requireAuth,
89:   requireRole
90: };

</content>
