<content>1: const Koa = require("koa");
2: const bodyParser = require("koa-bodyparser");
3: const request = require("supertest");
4: const requestId = require("../src/middleware/request-id");
5: const errorHandler = require("../src/middleware/error-handler");
6: const requestRefundMock = vi.fn();
7: const refundServicePath = require.resolve("../src/modules/payments/refunds.service");
8: 
9: require.cache[refundServicePath] = {
10:   id: refundServicePath,
11:   filename: refundServicePath,
12:   loaded: true,
13:   exports: { requestRefund: requestRefundMock }
14: };
15: 
16: const paymentsRoutes = require("../src/modules/payments/payments.routes");
17: 
18: function buildApp() {
19:   const app = new Koa();
20:   app.use(errorHandler);
21:   app.use(requestId);
22:   app.use(bodyParser({ enableTypes: ["json"] }));
23:   app.use(async (ctx, next) => {
24:     const userId = ctx.get("x-test-user-id");
25:     if (userId) {
26:       ctx.state.user = {
27:         id: Number(userId),
28:         username: ctx.get("x-test-username") || "test-user",
29:         email: "test@example.local",
30:         status: "active",
31:         sessionId: 1,
32:         roles: (ctx.get("x-test-user-roles") || "")
33:           .split(",")
34:           .map((role) => role.trim())
35:           .filter(Boolean)
36:       };
37:     }
38:     await next();
39:   });
40: 
41:   app.use(paymentsRoutes.routes());
42:   app.use(paymentsRoutes.allowedMethods());
43:   return app;
44: }
45: 
46: describe("Refund authorization", () => {
47:   beforeEach(() => {
48:     requestRefundMock.mockReset();
49:     requestRefundMock.mockResolvedValue({ id: 77, refund_status: "processed", amount_cents: 100 });
50:   });
51: 
52:   afterAll(() => {
53:     delete require.cache[refundServicePath];
54:     delete require.cache[require.resolve("../src/modules/payments/payments.routes")];
55:   });
56: 
57:   test("unauthenticated refund request returns 401", async () => {
58:     const app = buildApp();
59:     const response = await request(app.callback())
60:       .post("/api/v1/payments/orders/101/refunds")
61:       .send({ amountDollars: 1.0, reason: "test", idempotencyKey: "key001" });
62: 
63:     expect(response.status).toBe(401);
64:     expect(response.body.success).toBe(false);
65:     expect(response.body.error.code).toBe("UNAUTHORIZED");
66:     expect(requestRefundMock).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
67:   });
68: 
69:   test("authenticated non-privileged user refund request returns 403", async () => {
70:     const app = buildApp();
71:     const response = await request(app.callback())
72:       .post("/api/v1/payments/orders/101/refunds")
73:       .set("x-test-user-id", "20")
74:       .set("x-test-user-roles", "user")
75:       .send({ amountDollars: 1.0, reason: "test", idempotencyKey: "key002" });
76: 
77:     expect(response.status).toBe(403);
78:     expect(response.body.success).toBe(false);
79:     expect(response.body.error.code).toBe("FORBIDDEN");
80:     expect(requestRefundMock).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
81:   });
82: 
83:   test("cross-user style attempt by non-privileged actor returns 403", async () => {
84:     const app = buildApp();
85:     const response = await request(app.callback())
86:       .post("/api/v1/payments/orders/999/refunds")
87:       .set("x-test-user-id", "21")
88:       .set("x-test-user-roles", "user")
89:       .send({ amountDollars: 0.5, reason: "cross-user", idempotencyKey: "key003" });
90: 
91:     expect(response.status).toBe(403);
92:     expect(response.body.success).toBe(false);
93:     expect(response.body.error.code).toBe("FORBIDDEN");
94:     expect(requestRefundMock).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
95:   });
96: 
97:   test("authorized support/admin role refund request succeeds", async () => {
98:     const app = buildApp();
99:     const response = await request(app.callback())
100:       .post("/api/v1/payments/orders/202/refunds")
101:       .set("x-test-user-id", "2")
102:       .set("x-test-user-roles", "support")
103:       .send({ amountDollars: 1.25, reason: "approved", idempotencyKey: "key004" });
104: 
105:     expect(response.status).toBe(201);
106:     expect(response.body.success).toBe(true);
107:     expect(requestRefundMock).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1);
108:     expect(requestRefundMock.mock.calls[0][0]).toMatchObject({
109:       orderId: 202,
110:       actorUserId: 2,
111:       actorRoles: ["support"]
112:     });
113:   });

<type>file</type>
<content>1: <template>
2:   <FeedPanel
3:     :items="items"
4:     :loading="loading"
5:     :error="error"
6:     :preferences="preferences"
7:     :current-user-id="currentUserId"
8:     :followed-author-ids="followedAuthorIds"
9:     :follow-loading-ids="followLoadingIds"
10:     :action-loading-keys="actionLoadingKeys"
11:     :prefs-saving="prefsSaving"
12:     :showing-cached-feed="showingCachedFeed"
13:     :showing-cached-preferences="showingCachedPreferences"
14:     @refresh="loadFeed"
15:     @action="handleAction"
16:     @toggle-follow="handleToggleFollow"
17:     @save-preferences="savePreferences"
18:   />
19: </template>
20: 
21: <script setup>
22: import { computed, onMounted, ref } from "vue";
23: import { useSession } from "../session";
24: import FeedPanel from "../components/FeedPanel.vue";
25: import { getFeed, getFeedPreferences, updateFeedPreferences, sendFeedAction, listMyFollows, followUser, unfollowUser } from "../api";
26: import { useToast } from "../toast";
27: import {
28:   saveFeedSnapshot,
29:   loadFeedSnapshot,
30:   saveFeedPreferencesSnapshot,
31:   loadFeedPreferencesSnapshot
32: } from "../offline/persistence";
33: import { isLikelyOfflineError, recordOfflineIntent, buildOfflineRetryMessage } from "../offline/mutation-intents";
34: 
35: const items = ref([]);
36: const loading = ref(false);
37: const error = ref("");
38: const preferences = ref(null);
39: const prefsSaving = ref(false);
40: const followedAuthorIds = ref([]);
41: const followLoadingIds = ref([]);
42: const actionLoadingKeys = ref([]);
43: const showingCachedFeed = ref(false);
44: const showingCachedPreferences = ref(false);
45: const { state } = useSession();
46: const currentUserId = computed(() => Number(state.user?.id || 0));
47: 
48: const { pushToast } = useToast();
49: 
50: async function loadPreferences() {
51:   try {
52:     const response = await getFeedPreferences();
53:     preferences.value = response.data;
54:     saveFeedPreferencesSnapshot(currentUserId.value, preferences.value);
55:     showingCachedPreferences.value = false;
56:   } catch (err) {
57:     const cached = loadFeedPreferencesSnapshot(currentUserId.value);
58:     if (!cached?.data) {
59:       throw err;
60:     }
61:     preferences.value = cached.data;
62:     showingCachedPreferences.value = true;
63:   }
64: }
65: 
66: async function loadFollows() {
67:   const response = await listMyFollows();
68:   followedAuthorIds.value = (response.data || []).map((row) => Number(row.user_id));
69: }
70: 
71: async function loadFeed() {
72:   loading.value = true;
73:   error.value = "";
74:   try {
75:     const response = await getFeed(30);
76:     items.value = response.data || [];
77:     saveFeedSnapshot(currentUserId.value, items.value);
78:     showingCachedFeed.value = false;
79:   } catch (err) {
80:     const cached = loadFeedSnapshot(currentUserId.value);
81:     if (cached?.data?.length) {
82:       items.value = cached.data;
83:       showingCachedFeed.value = true;
84:       error.value = "";
85:     } else {
86:       error.value = err.message || "Failed to load feed";
87:     }
88:   } finally {
89:     loading.value = false;
90:   }
91: }
92: 
93: async function handleAction(payload) {
94:   if (actionLoadingKeys.value.includes(payload.localItemKey)) {
95:     return;
96:   }
97: 
98:   actionLoadingKeys.value = [...actionLoadingKeys.value, payload.localItemKey];
99:   const snapshot = [...items.value];
100:   const shouldRemoveImmediately = payload.action !== "clicked";
101:   if (shouldRemoveImmediately) {
102:     items.value = items.value.filter((item) => `${item.type}:${item.id}` !== payload.localItemKey);
103:   }
104:   pushToast(payload.action === "clicked" ? "Interaction saved" : "Feed updated", "success");
105:   try {
106:     await sendFeedAction(payload);
107:   } catch (err) {
108:     if (shouldRemoveImmediately) {
109:       items.value = snapshot;
110:     }
111:     if (isLikelyOfflineError(err)) {
112:       recordOfflineIntent("feed_action", {
113:         action: payload.action,
114:         itemType: payload.itemType,
115:         localItemKey: payload.localItemKey
116:       });
117:       pushToast(buildOfflineRetryMessage("Feed action"), "error");
118:     } else {
119:       pushToast(err.message || "Action failed", "error");
120:     }
121:   } finally {
122:     actionLoadingKeys.value = actionLoadingKeys.value.filter((key) => key !== payload.localItemKey);
123:   }
124: }
125: 
126: async function savePreferences(payload) {
127:   prefsSaving.value = true;
128:   try {
129:     const response = await updateFeedPreferences(payload);
130:     preferences.value = response.data;
131:     saveFeedPreferencesSnapshot(currentUserId.value, preferences.value);
132:     showingCachedPreferences.value = false;
133:     pushToast("Preferences saved", "success");
134:     await loadFeed();
135:   } catch (err) {
136:     if (isLikelyOfflineError(err)) {
137:       const optimistic = { ...(preferences.value || {}), ...payload };
138:       preferences.value = optimistic;
139:       saveFeedPreferencesSnapshot(currentUserId.value, optimistic);
140:       showingCachedPreferences.value = true;
141:       recordOfflineIntent("feed_preferences_update");
142:       pushToast(buildOfflineRetryMessage("Preferences update"), "error");
143:     } else {
144:       pushToast(err.message || "Failed to save preferences", "error");
145:     }
146:   } finally {
147:     prefsSaving.value = false;
148:   }
149: }
150: 
151: async function handleToggleFollow({ authorUserId, authorName, currentlyFollowed }) {
152:   if (!authorUserId) {
153:     return;
154:   }
155:   followLoadingIds.value = [...followLoadingIds.value, authorUserId];
156:   const previous = [...followedAuthorIds.value];
157:   if (currentlyFollowed) {
158:     followedAuthorIds.value = followedAuthorIds.value.filter((id) => id !== authorUserId);
159:   } else {
160:     followedAuthorIds.value = [...new Set([...followedAuthorIds.value, authorUserId])];
161:   }
162: 
163:   try {
164:     if (currentlyFollowed) {
165:       await unfollowUser(authorUserId);
166:       pushToast(`Unfollowed ${authorName}`, "success");
167:     } else {
168:       await followUser(authorUserId);
169:       pushToast(`Following ${authorName}`, "success");
170:     }
171:     await loadFeed();
172:   } catch (err) {
173:     followedAuthorIds.value = previous;
174:     if (isLikelyOfflineError(err)) {
175:       recordOfflineIntent("follow_toggle", { authorUserId });
176:       pushToast(buildOfflineRetryMessage("Follow update"), "error");
177:     } else {
178:       pushToast(err.message || "Failed to update follow", "error");
179:     }
180:   } finally {
181:     followLoadingIds.value = followLoadingIds.value.filter((id) => id !== authorUserId);
182:   }
183: }
184: 
185: onMounted(async () => {
186:   try {
187:     await loadPreferences();
188:     await loadFollows();
189:   } catch (err) {
190:     error.value = err.message || "Failed to load preferences";
191:   }
192:   await loadFeed();
193: });
194: </script>

files.before:
# Frontend (TrailForge)

This frontend is a Vue 3 + Vite app with route-level auth/RBAC, business flows (feed, reviews, orders, activities), and offline-friendly behavior.

## Commands

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
npm run test
npm run build
npm run preview
```

## Test Strategy

- Unit/API tests validate shared request behavior (including 401/403 error propagation).
- Route guard tests validate auth/guest/RBAC navigation policy in `src/router.js`.
- Page integration tests cover critical success and failure paths:
  - feed optimistic action + rollback + duplicate-click protection,
  - review create + image validation edges + follow-up/appeal flow protections,
  - orders refund/complete success and error handling.
- Existing app-shell test remains as a baseline smoke check.

## Offline Strategy

Offline behavior is intentionally simple and bounded:

1. **Service Worker (`public/sw.js`)**
   - App shell cache for offline reload (`/`, `/index.html`).
   - Runtime cache for same-origin static assets.
   - Authenticated/private API responses are not cached in the service worker.

2. **Client persistence (`src/offline/persistence.js`)**
   - Stores last feed snapshot and feed preferences in `localStorage`.
   - Feed page falls back to cached data when network requests fail.
   - Cached-mode indicator is shown in the feed UI.

3. **Offline mutation UX (`src/offline/mutation-intents.js`)**
   - Failed offline mutation attempts create local retry intents.
   - UI shows pending retry count and clear action.
   - User gets explicit retry messaging (no silent failure).

4. **Account-switch isolation (`src/offline/private-data.js`)**
   - Logout and user/session transitions clear persisted private offline state.
   - Service worker receives a private-cache clear message to purge legacy private API cache buckets.

## Verification Boundaries

- Backend authorization/business rules are still backend-owned and must be verified against a running backend.
- Offline mutation intents are not auto-replayed; users retry manually after reconnecting.
- Binary upload durability for offline review image uploads is not persisted across full browser restarts.
- E2E tests mock API responses at browser level; backend data integrity remains backend-suite responsibility.

See `verification.md` for reproducible acceptance checks.
files.after:
# Frontend (TrailForge)

This frontend is a Vue 3 + Vite app with route-level auth/RBAC, business flows (feed, reviews, orders, activities), and offline-friendly behavior.

## Commands

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
npm run test
npm run build
npm run preview
npm run e2e
```

## Test Strategy

- Unit/API tests validate shared request behavior (including 401/403 error propagation).
- Route guard tests validate auth/guest/RBAC navigation policy in `src/router.js`.
- Page integration tests cover critical success and failure paths:
  - feed optimistic action + rollback + duplicate-click protection,
  - review create + image validation edges + follow-up/appeal flow protections,
  - orders refund/complete success and error handling.
- Existing app-shell test remains as a baseline smoke check.
- Playwright E2E covers auth route-guard and a core feed action journey.

## Offline Strategy

Offline behavior is intentionally simple and bounded:

1. **Service Worker (`public/sw.js`)**
   - App shell cache for offline reload (`/`, `/index.html`).
   - Runtime cache for same-origin static assets.
   - Authenticated/private API responses are not cached in the service worker.

2. **Client persistence (`src/offline/persistence.js`)**
   - Stores last feed snapshot and feed preferences in `localStorage`.
   - Feed page falls back to cached data when network requests fail.
   - Cached-mode indicator is shown in the feed UI.

3. **Offline mutation UX (`src/offline/mutation-intents.js`)**
   - Failed offline mutation attempts create local retry intents.
   - UI shows pending retry count and clear action.
   - User gets explicit retry messaging (no silent failure).

4. **Account-switch isolation (`src/offline/private-data.js`)**
   - Logout and user/session transitions clear persisted private offline state.
   - Service worker receives a private-cache clear message to purge legacy private API cache buckets.

## Verification Boundaries

- Backend authorization/business rules are still backend-owned and must be verified against a running backend.
- Offline mutation intents are not auto-replayed; users retry manually after reconnecting.
- Binary upload durability for offline review image uploads is not persisted across full browser restarts.
- E2E tests mock API responses at browser level; backend data integrity remains backend-suite responsibility.

See `verification.md` for reproducible acceptance checks, including unit + E2E commands.

+# Frontend Verification Guide
+
+This guide provides reproducible local checks for the frontend without requiring Docker.
+
+## Prerequisites
+
+- Node.js 18+
+- npm
+
+From `repo/frontend`:
+
+```bash
+npm install
+```
+
+## 1) Unit and component tests
+
+```bash
+npm run test
+```
+
+Expected result:
+
+- Vitest completes with passing tests.
+
+## 2) Production build check
+
+```bash
+npm run build
+```
+
+Expected result:
+
+- Vite build succeeds and writes `dist/`.
+
+## 3) Minimal E2E check (Playwright)
+
+Install browser once:
+
+```bash
+npm run e2e:install
+```
+
+Run E2E suite:
+
+```bash
+npm run e2e
+```
+
+What this verifies:
+
+- Guest visiting a protected route is redirected to `/login`.
+- Login flow succeeds via API mocks.
+- User reaches feed and can perform a core feed action (`Not Interested`) with UI state update.
+
+## 4) Optional manual smoke run
+
+```bash
+npm run dev
+```
+
+Then open `http://localhost:5173` and verify:
+
+- Login page appears when unauthenticated.
+- After sign-in, sidebar and feed render.
+
+## Exit behavior
+
+- `npm run test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run e2e` return non-zero on failure.
+- These commands are safe to run repeatedly.
*** End Patch"}

# Frontend Verification Guide

This guide provides reproducible local checks for the frontend without requiring Docker.

## Prerequisites

- Node.js 18+
- npm

From `repo/frontend`:

```bash
npm install
```

## 1) Unit and component tests

```bash
npm run test
```

Expected result:

- Vitest completes with passing tests.

## 2) Production build check

```bash
npm run build
```

Expected result:

- Vite build succeeds and writes `dist/`.

## 3) Minimal E2E check (Playwright)

Install browser once:

```bash
npm run e2e:install
```

Run E2E suite:

```bash
npm run e2e
```

What this verifies:

- Guest visiting a protected route is redirected to `/login`.
- Login flow succeeds via API mocks.
- User reaches feed and can perform a core feed action (`Not Interested`) with UI state update.

## 4) Optional manual smoke run

```bash
npm run dev
```

Then open `http://localhost:5173` and verify:

- Login page appears when unauthenticated.
- After sign-in, sidebar and feed render.

## Exit behavior

- `npm run test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run e2e` return non-zero on failure.
- These commands are safe to run repeatedly.

content.text:
Implemented all requested fixes in `repo` only, with focused changes and no Docker usage.

**What I changed**

- **Backend auth fix for `GET /api/v1/reviews/:id`**
  - Added review-detail object auth helper in `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.authorization.js`
    - `ensureCanAccessReviewDetail({ review, requester })`
    - Allows only owner or privileged role (`admin`, `support`, `coach`)
    - Throws `403 FORBIDDEN` for authenticated but unauthorized users
  - Enforced this check in `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.read.service.js` inside `getReviewDetail` immediately after loading the review row (before loading related detail rows), preserving authorized behavior and arbitration visibility behavior.

- **Backend tests for security coverage**
  - Added API authz test suite: `backend/tests/review-detail-authz.api.test.js`
    - owner can access (200)
    - privileged role can access (200)
    - non-owner regular user denied (403)
    - non-authenticated denied (401)
  - Expanded service-level coverage in `backend/tests/reviews-read.service.test.js`
    - privileged access allowed
    - regular non-owner denied with `FORBIDDEN`
    - asserts unauthorized path short-circuits further detail queries

- **Frontend docs mismatch fix**
  - Added missing `frontend/verification.md` with reproducible local verification steps (unit tests, build, E2E, optional manual smoke).
  - Updated `frontend/README.md` to include E2E command and align with actual repo scripts.

- **Frontend E2E setup + critical journey**
  - Added Playwright config: `frontend/playwright.config.js`
  - Added E2E test: `frontend/e2e/auth-feed.spec.js`
    - verifies route guard redirect (guest hitting protected route -> `/login`)
    - verifies login flow and core feed action (`Not Interested`) updates UI state
  - Updated scripts in `frontend/package.json`:
    - `e2e`
    - `e2e:headed`
    - `e2e:install`
  - Updated `frontend/vite.config.js` test config to avoid Vitest collecting E2E files / node_modules specs:
    - `include: ["src/**/*.test.{js,ts}"]`
    - `exclude: ["e2e/**"]`

**Command results**

- **Backend tests**
  - Command: `npm --prefix backend test`
  - Result: Γ£à passed (`25` files, `75` tests)

- **Frontend tests**
  - Command: `npm --prefix frontend test`
  - Result: Γ£à passed (`10` files, `21` tests)

- **Frontend build**
  - Command: `npm --prefix frontend run build`
  - Result: Γ£à passed

- **New E2E command**
  - Install command: `npm --prefix frontend run e2e:install`
  - Run command: `npm --prefix frontend run e2e`
  - Result: Γ£à passed (`1` test)

**Concise changelog by issue**

1) **Backend security bug**
- `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.authorization.js`
- `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.read.service.js`
- Fix: object-level auth for review detail route behavior now enforced at service boundary; unauthorized authenticated users receive `403 FORBIDDEN`.

2) **Missing backend coverage**
- `backend/tests/review-detail-authz.api.test.js`
- `backend/tests/reviews-read.service.test.js`
- Fix: owner/privileged/non-owner/unauthenticated cases now covered.

3) **Frontend doc mismatch**
- `frontend/verification.md` (new)
- `frontend/README.md` (updated)
- Fix: missing referenced doc added with accurate local verification instructions.

4) **Frontend E2E gap**
- `frontend/playwright.config.js` (new)
- `frontend/e2e/auth-feed.spec.js` (new)
- `frontend/package.json` / `frontend/package-lock.json` (updated)
- `frontend/vite.config.js` (updated)
- Fix: minimal Playwright setup + meaningful critical journey test added and documented.
Remove the verification file it is not needed, remove the file and the documentation mentioning it

# Frontend Verification Guide

This guide provides reproducible local checks for the frontend without requiring Docker.

## Prerequisites

- Node.js 18+
- npm

From `repo/frontend`:

```bash
npm install
```

## 1) Unit and component tests

```bash
npm run test
```

Expected result:

- Vitest completes with passing tests.

## 2) Production build check

```bash
npm run build
```

Expected result:

- Vite build succeeds and writes `dist/`.

## 3) Minimal E2E check (Playwright)

Install browser once:

```bash
npm run e2e:install
```

Run E2E suite:

```bash
npm run e2e
```

What this verifies:

- Guest visiting a protected route is redirected to `/login`.
- Login flow succeeds via API mocks.
- User reaches feed and can perform a core feed action (`Not Interested`) with UI state update.

## 4) Optional manual smoke run

```bash
npm run dev
```

Then open `http://localhost:5173` and verify:

- Login page appears when unauthenticated.
- After sign-in, sidebar and feed render.

## Exit behavior

- `npm run test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run e2e` return non-zero on failure.
- These commands are safe to run repeatedly.

# Frontend (TrailForge)

This frontend is a Vue 3 + Vite app with route-level auth/RBAC, business flows (feed, reviews, orders, activities), and offline-friendly behavior.

## Commands

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
npm run test
npm run build
npm run preview
npm run e2e
```

## Test Strategy

- Unit/API tests validate shared request behavior (including 401/403 error propagation).
- Route guard tests validate auth/guest/RBAC navigation policy in `src/router.js`.
- Page integration tests cover critical success and failure paths:
  - feed optimistic action + rollback + duplicate-click protection,
  - review create + image validation edges + follow-up/appeal flow protections,
  - orders refund/complete success and error handling.
- Existing app-shell test remains as a baseline smoke check.
- Playwright E2E covers auth route-guard and a core feed action journey.

## Offline Strategy

Offline behavior is intentionally simple and bounded:

1. **Service Worker (`public/sw.js`)**
   - App shell cache for offline reload (`/`, `/index.html`).
   - Runtime cache for same-origin static assets.
   - Authenticated/private API responses are not cached in the service worker.

2. **Client persistence (`src/offline/persistence.js`)**
   - Stores last feed snapshot and feed preferences in `localStorage`.
   - Feed page falls back to cached data when network requests fail.
   - Cached-mode indicator is shown in the feed UI.

3. **Offline mutation UX (`src/offline/mutation-intents.js`)**
   - Failed offline mutation attempts create local retry intents.
   - UI shows pending retry count and clear action.
   - User gets explicit retry messaging (no silent failure).

4. **Account-switch isolation (`src/offline/private-data.js`)**
   - Logout and user/session transitions clear persisted private offline state.
   - Service worker receives a private-cache clear message to purge legacy private API cache buckets.

## Verification Boundaries

- Backend authorization/business rules are still backend-owned and must be verified against a running backend.
- Offline mutation intents are not auto-replayed; users retry manually after reconnecting.
- Binary upload durability for offline review image uploads is not persisted across full browser restarts.
- E2E tests mock API responses at browser level; backend data integrity remains backend-suite responsibility.

See `verification.md` for reproducible acceptance checks, including unit + E2E commands.
files.after:

   - Cached-mode indicator is shown in the feed UI.

3. **Offline mutation UX (`src/offline/mutation-intents.js`)**
   - Failed offline mutation attempts create local retry intents.
   - UI shows pending retry count and clear action.
   - User gets explicit retry messaging (no silent failure).

4. **Account-switch isolation (`src/offline/private-data.js`)**
   - Logout and user/session transitions clear persisted private offline state.
   - Service worker receives a private-cache clear message to purge legacy private API cache buckets.

## Verification Boundaries

- Backend authorization/business rules are still backend-owned and must be verified against a running backend.
- Offline mutation intents are not auto-replayed; users retry manually after reconnecting.
- Binary upload durability for offline review image uploads is not persisted across full browser restarts.
- E2E tests mock API responses at browser level; backend data integrity remains backend-suite responsibility.

Use the commands above for reproducible local unit, build, and E2E checks.

Sensitive default environment secrets should not be allowed in non-dev environments.
Review service is overly large and should be modularized for maintainability.

content.text:
Yes ΓÇö those five review blockers are now addressed in this repo.

- Feed dedupe now excludes all seen/shown signals in last 7 days (not just negative actions), with query-level filtering and mixed-signal dedupe support in `backend/src/modules/feed/feed.service.js` and `backend/src/modules/feed/feed.logic.js`; covered by `backend/tests/feed-logic.test.js` and `backend/tests/feed-service-dedupe.test.js`.
- Review image object auth gap is fixed in `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.image.service.js` + `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.authorization.js`; covered by `backend/tests/review-image-authz.service.test.js`.
- Test coverage for key areas was expanded:
  - ingestion: `backend/tests/ingestion.service.test.js`
  - analytics: `backend/tests/analytics-logic.test.js`
  - object-level auth: `backend/tests/review-image-authz.service.test.js`, `backend/tests/review-detail-authz.api.test.js`, `backend/tests/reviews-read.service.test.js`
- Non-dev default secret validation is enforced via `backend/src/config/env.validation.js`, wired in `backend/src/config/env.js`; covered by `backend/tests/env-validation.test.js`.
- Review service was modularized into focused modules:
  - `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.create.service.js`
  - `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.followup.service.js`
  - `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.read.service.js`
  - `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.appeals.service.js`
  - `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.replies.service.js`
  - shared helpers in `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.shared.js`
  - compatibility surface preserved via `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.core.service.js` and `backend/src/modules/reviews/reviews.service.js`

Verification just rerun:
- `npm --prefix backend test` Γ£à passed (`25` files, `75` tests). 

If you want, I can also rerun frontend `test/build/e2e` now and paste a fresh full acceptance snapshot.
